\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Imager}\blockicon{stereo}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Imager}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[STR]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: L, R}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Multiband stereo imager (Ozone-Imager-style): mono the low end and widen the highs - low frequencies (below Freq) are summed to the centre so the bass stays tight and phase-coherent, while the side content above Freq is spread by Width for an airy top. Low directional bass widened only weakens and phase-cancels the low end, so this keeps the 'stereo rose' shape: narrow at the bottom, widest at the top. Freq sets the mono crossover (80-120 Hz typical), Width the high-band spread (1 = unchanged, >1 wider, 0 fully mono), and Out picks the channel. in0 = L, in1 = R; run two instances on opposite Out for the stereo pair.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidSide}\blockicon{stereo}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidSide}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[STR]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: L, R}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Mid/side processor with independent gain on each component: splits L/R into mid and side, applies MidGain and SideGain in dB, then recombines. Lowering MidGain or raising SideGain widens the image while the reverse focuses the centre. Out picks L or R for the recombined channel; handy for level-matching centre versus stereo content.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MonoMaker}\blockicon{stereo}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MonoMaker}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[STR]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: L, R}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Bass mono-maker: a one-pole low-pass at Freq splits the spectrum so content below the crossover is summed to mono while the highs stay in their own channel. Freq sets the crossover from 20 to 500 Hz. Out picks L or R, supplying that channel's highs over the shared mono low end; tightens low-frequency phase for vinyl and club playback.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Pan}\blockicon{stereo}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Pan}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[STR]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Static constant-power panner for a mono source: places the input at a fixed position from -1 (hard left) to +1 (hard right) using an equal-power (sin/cos) law so perceived loudness stays constant across the sweep. The plain manual counterpart to the LFO-driven AutoPan and the frequency-split FreqPanner - set a source's place in the image once. Out picks L or R, supplying that channel's pan-law leg; centred (Pan 0) sends equal power to both.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Panner}\blockicon{stereo}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Panner}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[STR]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Constant-power panner for a mono source: maps Pan (-1 to +1) onto a sine/cosine gain pair so total power stays even across the field. Pan at 0 centres the source; the +/-1 extremes hard-pan it. Out picks L or R, each instance applying the matching sine or cosine leg of the pan law.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Rotate}\blockicon{stereo}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Rotate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[STR]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: L, R}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Stereo field rotation: rotates the L/R signal vector by Angle, tilting the whole stereo image (a hard-left source swings toward centre and on toward the right) - a true rotation rather than a width change. Angle sets the rotation in degrees and Out picks L or R. Subtle angles re-balance a lopsided image; +/-90 swaps the channels.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Shuffle}\blockicon{stereo}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Shuffle}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[STR]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: L, R}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Blumlein shuffler (frequency-dependent stereo width): splits the side (L-R) signal at a crossover and applies Bass width below and Treble width above, so you can collapse the low end toward mono for a tight, phase-safe centre while widening the highs - the classic mastering width control. Freq sets the crossover, Bass/Treble the width on each side of it, and Out picks L or R.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:StereoImager}\blockicon{stereo}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{StereoImager}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[STR]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: L, R}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Frequency-dependent imager: splits the side signal at Freq with a one-pole low-pass and scales only the high side band by Width, leaving the low side untouched. Width sets the high-band spread and Freq sets the split point from 100 to 2000 Hz. Out picks L or R for the recombined channel; widens air and detail while keeping the low end mono-safe.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Stereoize}\blockicon{stereo}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Stereoize}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[STR]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Mono-to-stereo decorrelator: runs the input through a four-stage all-pass chain to phase-shift it without changing tone, then blends that toward the dry signal by Amount. Amount controls how much decorrelated signal is mixed in. Out picks L (dry) or R (decorrelated) so the stereo pair widens a mono source while keeping a centred reference.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:StereoWidth}\blockicon{stereo}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{StereoWidth}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[STR]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: L, R}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Mid/side stereo widener: decomposes the L/R pair into mid (sum) and side (difference), scales the side by Width, then recombines. Width below 1 narrows toward mono and above 1 (up to 2) exaggerates the stereo spread. Out picks L or R, so one instance emits each recombined channel; useful for tightening or opening a stereo bus.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

## Utility (147)

\phantomsection\label{blk:Abs}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Abs}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Absolute value / full-wave rectifier: outputs |in0| * Gain plus Offset, so

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AbsDiff}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AbsDiff}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Unsigned difference: outputs |in0 - in1| * Scale, the distance between the

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AbsDiffSq}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AbsDiffSq}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Squared difference: (a-b)^2 scaled, which is zero when the two inputs match and grows quadratically as they diverge - a sharp similarity/error detector for sidechaining, gating on signal mismatch, or driving modulation from how far apart two sources are.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AbsMax}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AbsMax}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Largest magnitude: outputs the greater of |in0| and |in1| times Gain - the

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AbsSum}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AbsSum}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sum of magnitudes: |a|+|b| scaled, the combined rectified level of two sources - a quick total-energy/activity signal for driving envelopes, gates or meters from a pair of inputs, distinct from a signed mix or a max-of-magnitudes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Acos}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Acos}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Arccosine shaper: clamps in0 to [-1, 1] then outputs acos(in0) / pi, a smooth

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Analyzer}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Analyzer}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Signal analyzer: passes in 1 straight through (out = in 1, transparent in the chain) while its detail panel shows a live 3D FFT waterfall + oscilloscope of in 1, and a Lissajous (X-Y) phase scope of in 1 against in 2. A measurement tool, not an effect - drop it inline to see the spectrum feeding a filter, the harmonics a distortion adds, what an envelope follower tracks, or the stereo/phase relationship of two signals.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ArgMax}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ArgMax}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Winner-take-all index: outputs which of in0..in3 is currently the largest, as

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Asin}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Asin}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Arcsine shaper: clamps in0 to [-1, 1] then outputs asin(in0) * 2/pi, an odd

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Asinh}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Asinh}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Inverse-hyperbolic-sine compander: outputs asinh(Drive * in0) / asinh(Drive), a

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Atan}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Atan}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Arctangent shaper: outputs (2/pi) * atan(Drive * in0), a smooth odd S-curve that

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Atan2}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Atan2}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Two-argument arctangent: outputs the angle of the (in0, in1) vector mapped to

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Atanh}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Atanh}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Inverse-hyperbolic-tangent expander: clamps in0 to +/-Edge then outputs

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Attenuvert}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Attenuvert}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Attenuverter + offset: scales the input by a bipolar gain (can invert) and adds a DC offset - the fundamental CV-shaping utility for inverting, attenuating and biasing control or audio signals.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Average}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Average}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Mean of the four signal inputs, weighted only by how many are non-zero so

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BeatRepeat}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BeatRepeat}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Beat repeat: on each trigger it grabs the most recent slice and loops it for the slice length, freezing the groove into a glitchy stutter until the next trigger - the classic beat-repeat/roll effect.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Bernoulli}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Bernoulli}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Bernoulli gate (coin-flip router): on each rising edge of the in0 trigger it flips a weighted coin and either passes that gate through or blocks it, with Probability setting the odds. A stream of clocks becomes a randomly thinned-out pattern - the building block for generative rhythms and stochastic sequencing. Unlike the deterministic ClockDiv, every pulse is an independent chance event; the decision latches until the next trigger.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BitRepeat}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BitRepeat}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Fixed-rate sample-hold decimator: re-samples the input at a chosen rate and holds between samples (the deterministic, non-random cousin of a glitch hold) - classic downsampling aliasing and lo-fi character.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BrightCV}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BrightCV}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Brightness follower: compares the energy above a split frequency to the total energy and outputs the ratio, a cheap spectral-centroid-style 'how bright is it right now' control voltage for driving timbre-reactive patches.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Burst}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Burst}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Burst generator: a rising edge on in0 fires a burst of Count evenly-spaced trigger pulses at Rate, then falls silent until the next input edge - turn one hit into a ratchet, a drum-roll trigger or a stutter clock. Count sets how many pulses and Rate their spacing; re-triggering mid-burst restarts the count. Patch its output into envelopes, sample-and-holds or sequencer clocks.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CentroidalMean}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CentroidalMean}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Centroidal mean: outputs (2/3)(|a|^2+|a||b|+|b|^2)/(|a|+|b|), the x-coordinate of the centroid of the region under a line between the two values - another above-arithmetic mean with its own bias toward the larger input, distinct from the contraharmonic. Gain scales the result.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ChordQuant}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ChordQuant}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Chord quantizer: snaps in0 - a pitch in semitones (the Note source, a

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Clamp}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Clamp}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Hard range limiter: constrains in0 to the [Lo, Hi] window with no

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ClockDiv}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ClockDiv}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Clock divider: passes only every Div-th rising edge of the in0 trigger, turning a fast gate or clock into a slower one for polyrhythms and rhythmic gating. The output mirrors the input pulse on the pulses it lets through and is silent otherwise, and the count stays phase-locked to the input.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ClockMult}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ClockMult}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Clock}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Clock multiplier: measures the period of the in0 clock and emits Mult evenly-spaced pulses per input beat - the complement to ClockDiv, for faster subdivisions and ratchets. Locks to the input tempo on every edge so it tracks tempo changes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Comparator}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Comparator}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Comparator: outputs a gate that goes high when input A rises above input B and low when it falls below by a hysteresis margin - turns any signal into a clean trigger with no chatter near the threshold.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Compare}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Compare}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Schmitt comparator: outputs +1 (high) when in0 rises above

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Complement}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Complement}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Unit-range inverter: outputs Center - in0 * Scale, defaulting to 1 - in0 so

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Constant}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Constant}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

DC source: emits a fixed Value every sample with no input. Patch it

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ContraharmonicMean}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ContraharmonicMean}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Contraharmonic mean: outputs (|a|^2+|b|^2)/(|a|+|b|) of the two inputs, a mean that always lies above the arithmetic mean and is pulled toward whichever input is larger - useful for level-combining that favours the louder source. Gain scales the result.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CopySign}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CopySign}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Copy-sign: outputs the magnitude of A carrying the sign (polarity) of B - so B's zero-crossings/phase drive A's, useful for re-polarizing a signal, building ring-mod-like polarity gating, or forcing one source to follow another's sign pattern.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Correlate}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Correlate}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: A, B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Two-signal relatedness meter (a first as a synth block): outputs a running normalised cross-correlation of in0 against in1 - how strongly the two signals move together - as a control voltage. About +1 when they rise and fall in lock-step, 0 when independent, -1 when they mirror each other. Window sets the averaging time. Detect when two modulators line up, gate an effect on the agreement of two sources, or measure stereo/phase coherence. Both inputs must vary (a constant has no correlation).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Cosh}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Cosh}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Hyperbolic-cosine bowl: warps in0 through a normalised (cosh(in0 * Drive) - 1) /

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Counter}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Counter}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Trigger, Reset}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Pulse counter / staircase: each rising edge of the in0 trigger advances an internal count, and the output is that count scaled to 0..1 over Steps before wrapping to zero - a stepped CV ramp for sequences and rhythmic builds. A rising edge on in1 resets the count so it can be re-synced to a bar.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CrestCV}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CrestCV}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Crest-factor follower: outputs the ratio of peak level to RMS level, which is high for spiky percussive material and low for sustained tones - a 'how punchy is it' control voltage for transient-aware patching.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Crossfade}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Crossfade}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Blends from in0 to in1 by a fade position of Mix plus the in2 CV

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CrossfadeLoop}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CrossfadeLoop}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Crossfade looper: two overlapping read-heads replay the most recent window forward on a loop with triangular crossfades, so the captured texture repeats seamlessly with no click at the loop point - an instant stutter/loop layer.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CrossTrig}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CrossTrig}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Crossing detector: emits a one-sample High pulse each time in0 crosses the Level (zero by default), on the Rising, Falling or Both edge as selected. Turns an oscillator or LFO into a clock (a zero-crossing tick), derives a trigger from a gate's edge, or fires an event when a signal passes a threshold. Pairs with TrigToGate to stretch the pulse into a sustained gate.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CVBridge}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CVBridge}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Control-voltage utility that conditions a single input value in one of three Mode settings: attenuvert (scale by Scale plus Offset), note-to-1V/oct pitch conversion, or bipolar-to-unipolar remap. Scale provides positive or negative gain including inversion, and Offset shifts the result up or down. Useful for routing and rescaling modulation or pitch control between modules.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DCBlock}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DCBlock}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Removes DC offset and sub-sonic bias with a one-pole high-pass that subtracts the previous input and feeds back a 0.9995 coefficient, passing audio while draining any constant component. It takes no parameters and always runs at the same fixed corner. Place it after asymmetric distortion or rectification stages to keep the signal centred.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Deadband}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Deadband}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Centre dead-zone: passes in0 straight through once it moves more than Width

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Decibels}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Decibels}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Decibel <-> linear-gain converter. In dB->Gain mode it outputs 10^(in0 / 20),

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Dither}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Dither}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Bit-depth reducer with triangular (TPDF) dither: builds a quantization step from Bits, adds triangular noise from two xorshift draws scaled by Amount, then rounds to the step. Bits sets the target word length from 8 to 24 and Amount scales the dither level. The dither decorrelates quantization error so low-level detail survives reduction instead of distorting.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DitherCrush}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DitherCrush}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Dithered bit reducer: requantizes to a chosen bit depth after adding triangular (TPDF) dither, so the bit-crush decorrelates into a smooth noise floor instead of correlated distortion - lo-fi done the right way.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Divide}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Divide}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Safe divider: in0 divided by (in1 + Denom), with the result limited to

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Entropy}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Entropy}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Information-theoretic modulation source (a first as a synth block): outputs a running Shannon-entropy estimate of the input - near 0 for silence or a steady tone, rising toward 1 for noisy, unpredictable signals. It folds recent samples into a decaying 16-bin amplitude histogram and reports the normalised entropy of that distribution, answering 'how disordered is this signal right now?' as a control voltage. Window sets the memory (higher = slower, smoother). Patch it so an effect opens up only when the input turns chaotic.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Equal}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Equal}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tolerance comparator: outputs High when in0 sits within +/-Tol of (in1 plus

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Erf}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Erf}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Error-function sigmoid: outputs erf(Drive * in0), the Gaussian-integral S-curve

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Exp}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Exp}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Linear-to-exponential converter: outputs 2^(in0 * Range), turning a linear

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Expr}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Expr}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B, In C}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

User-written per-sample expression compiled by an RT-safe VM

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:FlipFlop}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{FlipFlop}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Trigger, Reset}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

T (toggle) flip-flop: each rising edge of the in0 trigger flips the output between 0 and 1, halving the clock and turning a stream of triggers into a square gate. A rising edge on in1 resets it to 0. The bistable building block for clock division and on/off latching.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Fold}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Fold}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Triangle wavefolder: when |in0 * Drive| exceeds Threshold the excess is

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Frac}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Frac}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Fractional part: outputs in0 minus its floor, the [0, 1) remainder after the

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Gain}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Gain}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Gain trim (dB) plus a DC bias offset.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GateDelay}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GateDelay}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Trigger}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Trigger delay: a rising edge on in0 is held for Time, then re-emitted as a short pulse - shift a trigger later in time to stagger hits, build echoes of a clock or offset a sequence. Each input edge arms an independent delay.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Gaussian}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Gaussian}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Bell-curve shaper: outputs exp(-((in0 - Centre)^2) / (2 * Width^2)), a smooth

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GeoMean}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GeoMean}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Geometric mean of two inputs: outputs sign(in0*in1) * sqrt(|in0 * in1|) *

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GiniMean}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GiniMean}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Gini mean: outputs ((a^R+b^R)/(a^S+b^S))^(1/(R-S)) of the two input magnitudes, a two-exponent generalization of the power means - R and S together place the result anywhere from harmonic-like to contraharmonic-like, the most flexible of the two-source mean combiners. Bounded.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GlitchHold}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GlitchHold}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sample-level glitch hold: with a set probability it freezes the current sample for a random run length, producing buffer-underrun-style digital stutter and aliasing artifacts (RT-safe deterministic RNG).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GrainCloud}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GrainCloud}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Granular cloud: continuously sprays short windowed grains plucked from random positions in the recent input buffer, smearing the source into an evolving textural cloud - the core of granular texture synthesis.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GranScrub}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GranScrub}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Granular scrubber: a position CV moves a windowed grain freely through the recent input buffer, so you can freeze, slow-scrub or jump around the recent audio in real time - tape-scrub/timestretch by hand.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GranTimeStretch}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GranTimeStretch}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Granular time stretch: overlapping grains advance their read position slower (or faster) than real time, stretching or compressing the recent audio's duration while leaving its pitch unchanged - time-domain timestretching.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:HeronianMean}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{HeronianMean}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Heronian mean: outputs (|a| + sqrt(|a||b|) + |b|)/3, the mean used for the volume of a frustum - it sits between the geometric and arithmetic means, a gentle blend of two sources that leans slightly toward their average. Gain scales the result.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:HilbertEnv}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{HilbertEnv}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Instantaneous amplitude: forms an approximate quadrature (90-degree) partner of the signal via an all-pass and takes the magnitude of the analytic signal, tracking the true envelope even within a single cycle - smoother than a rectified follower.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Hypot}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Hypot}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Vector magnitude: outputs sqrt(in0^2 + in1^2) scaled by Gain - the Euclidean

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:HypotDiff}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{HypotDiff}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Hypotenuse difference: outputs sqrt(max(0, a^2 - b^2)), the remaining leg of a right triangle given the hypotenuse A and one side B (and zero when B exceeds A) - a complement to the usual hypot, useful for de-correlation, sideband math or gating when one signal overtakes another. Gain scales the result.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Hysteresis}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Hysteresis}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Schmitt trigger with memory: the output latches High once in0 rises above the upper threshold (Center + Width) and stays High until in0 falls below the lower threshold (Center - Width), then latches Low. Because it remembers its last state, a noisy or slowly-drifting signal around one level produces a clean debounced gate instead of chattering - unlike the stateless Compare, which has no memory.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Integrator}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Integrator}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Signal, Reset}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Leaky integrator / accumulator (CV): sums in0 over time so a constant input ramps and a periodic input is smoothed and phase-shifted a quarter cycle. Rate scales the input before accumulation, Leak bleeds the accumulator toward zero (0 = a near-perfect integrator that holds, higher = a shorter-memory smoother), and a rising edge on in1 resets the sum to zero. The running output is safety-limited. Distinct from Slew (which rate-limits a signal) and Counter (which counts edges).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Latch}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Latch}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Bistable latch / flip-flop: remembers a High/Low state across time. In Toggle mode each rising edge on in0 flips the state (a clock divide-by-two, or tap-to-toggle). In Set/Reset mode a rising edge on in0 sets the output High and a rising edge on in1 sets it Low (a one-bit memory cell driven by two triggers). Outputs High when set and Low when clear - the missing fundamental logic block alongside Compare, Logic and Hysteresis.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:LehmerMean}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{LehmerMean}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Lehmer mean: outputs (|a|^p+|b|^p)/(|a|^(p-1)+|b|^(p-1)), a one-parameter family that gives the harmonic mean at Power=0, the arithmetic at 1 and the contraharmonic at 2 - so Power continuously biases the combination from the smaller toward the larger input.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:LmsCanceller}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{LmsCanceller}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

LMS adaptive canceller: an 8-tap adaptive FIR (least-mean-squares) that filters the reference input (in 2) to best match the correlated component of the primary input (in 1), then subtracts it - the classic adaptive setup for stripping hum, a tone, or any reference-correlated noise out of a signal. StepSize sets adaptation speed vs stability; the output is the residual error.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Log}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Log}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Exponential-to-linear converter: outputs log2(in0) / Range, the inverse of Exp -

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Logic}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Logic}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Boolean gate combiner: treats in0 and in1 as logic highs when they

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:LogicGate}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{LogicGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Gate logic: treats two inputs as boolean gates and outputs their AND, OR, XOR or NAND - the combinational-logic building block for deriving new triggers and rhythms from two existing gate streams.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Logistic}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Logistic}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Logistic sigmoid: outputs 1 / (1 + exp(-Gain * (in0 - Centre))), the classic

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Logit}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Logit}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Logit (inverse-logistic) expander: clamps in0 to [1-Edge, Edge] then outputs

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:LogMean}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{LogMean}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Logarithmic mean: outputs (|a|-|b|)/(ln|a|-ln|b|) (and |a| when they are equal), the mean from heat-transfer analysis that lies strictly between the geometric and arithmetic means - a smooth combiner biased toward the smaller value. Gain scales the result.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:LogSumExp}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{LogSumExp}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Smooth maximum: log(e^(k*a)+e^(k*b))/k, a differentiable soft-max of two signals that rounds the corner where a plain max would kink. Sharp sets how tightly it hugs the true maximum (high) versus averaging the two inputs (low) - a smooth selector/combiner.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Manhattan}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Manhattan}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Taxicab (L1) magnitude: outputs (|in0| + |in1|) * Gain, the sum of the two

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Map}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Map}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Affine range mapper: rescales in0 from its [In Lo, In Hi] range onto a

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Max}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Max}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Outputs the larger of in0 and in1 (after adding the In B offset to in1).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Median3}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Median3}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Median of three inputs: outputs the middle value of in0, in1, in2, discarding

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Meter}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Meter}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Analysis meter that tracks the running mean-square of the input and outputs its RMS level as a scalar control value. Smooth sets the one-pole averaging coefficient, with higher values giving slower, steadier readings and lower values reacting faster to transients. Used to derive a level signal for metering or to drive other modules from program loudness.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Min}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Min}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Outputs the smaller of in0 and in1 (after adding the In B offset to in1).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MinkowskiMix}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MinkowskiMix}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Minkowski (p-norm) mixer: combines two inputs by their L-p norm (|a|^Norm+|b|^Norm)^(1/Norm), carrying the sign of their sum - at Norm=1 it sums magnitudes, at Norm=2 it is the Euclidean/RMS blend, and as Norm grows it approaches a max() of the two. A tunable interpolation between additive and maximum mixing. Bounded.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MinMax}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MinMax}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: A, B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Minimum / maximum selector: outputs either the smaller or the larger of the two input voltages each sample - an analog comparator-style utility for combining envelopes, CVs or gates (max = OR-like, min = AND-like). Mode picks min or max. in0, in1 = inputs.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MinMaxGate}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MinMaxGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Two-input logic combiner: outputs the minimum, maximum or absolute difference of two signals - the analog-logic building block for combining CVs, rectifying differences or wave-multiplexing two sources.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MinMaxMorph}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MinMaxMorph}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Order-statistic morph: blends the sample-by-sample minimum and maximum of two inputs by Morph - at 0 it outputs min(a,b) (an AM-like duck to the quieter signal), at 1 it outputs max(a,b) (an OR-style combine to the louder), and at 0.5 their average. A continuous slider across the order statistics of two signals, distinct from additive or ring mixing.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Mixer}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Mixer}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B, In C}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sums three scaled inputs plus a DC offset.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Modulo}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Modulo}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Euclidean remainder: outputs in0 wrapped into [0, Mod), the non-

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Multiply}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Multiply}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Two-quadrant multiplier / VCA: in0 times in1 times Scale. With an

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Mux}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Mux}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Four-way input selector: routes one of in0..in3 to the output based on the

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Negate}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Negate}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sign inverter: outputs -in0 scaled by Scale (default 1) plus an Offset.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:NlmsCanceller}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{NlmsCanceller}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Normalized-LMS canceller: an 8-tap adaptive FIR like the plain LMS canceller, but the step is divided by the reference signal's instantaneous power - so convergence speed no longer depends on input level and it adapts faster and more stably across quiet and loud passages. Filters reference (in 2) to cancel its correlated part from primary (in 1); outputs the residual.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:NoiseRate}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{NoiseRate}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Noisiness follower: measures the zero-crossing rate of the signal (low for tones, high for noise) and outputs it as a control voltage, a tone-vs-noise discriminator for driving gates, filters or morphs.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:NullTest}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{NullTest}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: A, B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Null tester that subtracts the second input from the first and outputs the residual difference, scaled by Gain. When two signals are identical the output is silence, and any audible residual reveals the difference between them. Gain amplifies that residual so small mismatches become easy to hear, making this a tool for verifying bit-for-bit equivalence between processing chains.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PeakFollow}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PeakFollow}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Peak follower: tracks the instantaneous peak of the signal and decays back down at a set rate, a fast-attack/slow-release detector that produces a clean envelope CV for triggering or amplitude-following.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PeakHold}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PeakHold}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Peak / hold detector: latches the extreme value in0 reaches and holds it, optionally bleeding back toward zero at Decay (Decay 0 = hold forever). Mode picks the maximum (Peak), the minimum (Trough), or the largest magnitude (Abs Peak). A rising edge on the in1 reset clears the held value to the current input. Where EnvFollow smooths the amplitude with attack/release, PeakHold captures the strict extreme - peak-metering CV, grabbing the loudest transient, or a max-tracking sample-and-hold.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Periodicity}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Periodicity}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Self-similarity detector (a first as a synth block): writes the input to a delay line and outputs the running normalised correlation between the signal now and the signal one Period ago - how strongly it repeats itself at that lag. About 1 for a steady tone or loop whose cycle matches Period, near 0 for noise or a one-shot, so it reports the STRENGTH of repetition rather than the pitch (set Period to the cycle you are listening for). Window sets the averaging time. Drive an effect by how locked-in a groove is.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PhaseDiff}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PhaseDiff}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Wrapped phase distance: treats in0 and in1 as phases on a [-1, 1] cycle and

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PhaseInvert}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PhaseInvert}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Flips the polarity of the signal, negating every sample when engaged and passing it untouched otherwise. Invert toggles the inversion on or off. Use it to correct out-of-phase tracks or to null one source against another.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Power}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Power}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sign-preserving exponent: outputs sign(in0) * |in0|^Exp, so it reshapes

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PowerMean}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PowerMean}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Power mean: the generalized Holder mean of the two input magnitudes ((|a|^p+|b|^p)/2)^(1/p), where Power sweeps continuously through harmonic (-1), geometric (0), arithmetic (1) and RMS (2) means - one knob spanning every classical average for combining envelopes or control signals.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Quadratic}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Quadratic}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Second-order polynomial shaper: outputs A*in0^2 + B*in0 + C, a full parabola

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Quantize}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Quantize}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Rounds in0 to the nearest of Steps evenly-spaced levels across the

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Reciprocal}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Reciprocal}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

One-over-x: 1 / (in0 + Bias), limited to +/-Limit and floored away

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RectifyCV}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RectifyCV}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Rectifier: passes only the positive half, only the negative half, or the absolute value of the signal, then offsets it - the analog full/half-wave rectifier for deriving envelopes and folding control voltages.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RevGrain}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RevGrain}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Reverse-grain looper: continuously plays the most recent window of input backwards on a loop, layering a reversed echo of what just happened - granular reverse texture with no transport.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RMS3}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RMS3}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Root-mean-square of three inputs: outputs sqrt((in0^2 + in1^2 + in2^2) / 3) *

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RmsFollow}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RmsFollow}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

RMS follower: tracks the running root-mean-square of the signal rather than its peak, giving a smooth loudness-correlated envelope (the detector loudness meters and RMS compressors use) - steadier than a rectified peak follower.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Round}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Round}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Integer rounding with a selectable Mode (nearest, floor, ceil, truncate)

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SampHold}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SampHold}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sample and hold: captures the value of the signal input at the instant the trigger input rises and holds it until the next trigger - the classic stepped/random voltage source when fed noise and a clock.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SampleSkip}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SampleSkip}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Random sample dropout: with a set probability each sample is discarded and the previous one held, simulating a glitching digital link - bit-rot crackle and stutter, deterministic (RT-safe) RNG.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ScaleQuant}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ScaleQuant}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Musical scale / pitch quantizer: snaps in0 - read as a pitch in

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Select}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Select}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Two-way signal switch: routes in0 when the in2 selector (plus the

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Semitones}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Semitones}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Semitone-to-ratio pitch converter: outputs 2^((in0 * Range) / 12), turning a

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ShiftReg}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ShiftReg}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Clock, Signal}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Analog shift register: each rising edge of the in0 clock shifts the in1 voltage one stage down a chain, and the output is the value Stages steps ago - a tapped delay for CV that turns a melody or random source into canonic, echoing pitch patterns. The classic Buchla/Triadex-style sequencing trick.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Sign}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Sign}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sign extractor with a dead zone: outputs -1, 0, or +1 depending on

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SignGate}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SignGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Polarity transplant: outputs the magnitude of input A carrying the sign of input B - a one-bit ring modulator that imposes B's zero-crossing structure on A's amplitude.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Sinc}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Sinc}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Normalised sinc: outputs sin(pi * in0 * Scale) / (pi * in0 * Scale), the cardinal

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Sinh}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Sinh}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Hyperbolic-sine shaper: warps in0 through a normalised sinh(in0 * Drive) /

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Skew}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Skew}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Asymmetric curve bender: warps in0 across the [0,1] range so its midpoint slides

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SlewExp}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SlewExp}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Exponential slew limiter: limits how fast the output can follow, but with an exponential (RC-style) approach whose speed grows with the distance to the target - smooth glide / portamento with a natural curve, separate rise and fall.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SlewLimAsym}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SlewLimAsym}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Asymmetric slew limiter: caps how fast the output may rise and fall independently with a hard (linear, constant-slope) limit, for lag/portamento or attack-vs-release shaping with a straight-line edge rather than an exponential curve.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Slope}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Slope}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Signal}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Differentiator / rate-of-change detector (CV): outputs how fast in0 is moving - positive while it rises, negative while it falls, zero when steady - so it reads the slope of an envelope, LFO or any control signal.
