Sousaphone voice: the marching-band tuba, a very low rounded sawtooth through a dark lowpass with a soft breathy attack, the big oom-pah bass wrapped around the player. in0 = note, in1 = gate (breath). Octave/Fine tune it, Vibrato the waver, Dark the body, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SquareStack}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SquareStack}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM, Pitch Mod}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Detuned-square super-voice: seven band-limited pulse oscillators detuned around the note (a square-wave cousin of the supersaw) for a hollow, buzzy, hugely-wide stack that a single pulse cannot make. in0 = note, in1 = FM, in2 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Detune spreads the stack, PW the pulse width, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SteelGuitarVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SteelGuitarVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Steel-guitar voice: a plucked string that glides smoothly to each new note (the lap/pedal-steel slide), a Karplus pluck whose pitch portamentos from the previous note, with a warm sustain. in0 = note, in1 = gate (pluck). Octave/Fine tune it, Glide the slide time, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SteelpanVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SteelpanVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Steel-pan voice: the gate strikes a note-area of a Caribbean steel drum, a bright clustered stack of mostly-harmonic partials with a fast metallic attack and ringing sustain, the sunny tone of the pan. in0 = note, in1 = gate. Octave tunes it, Decay the ring, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:StringEnsembleVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{StringEnsembleVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

String-ensemble voice: a whole bowed section, several detuned sawtooth voices through a body lowpass with a slow swell and a built-in ensemble shimmer, the lush 'strings' pad. in0 = note, in1 = gate (bow). Octave tunes it, Spread the section width, Tone the cutoff, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:StutterVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{StutterVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Trance-gate voice: a detuned-saw tone chopped by a hard rhythmic on/off gate at the stutter rate with adjustable duty, the pumping gated-synth stab of trance and EDM. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Rate the gate speed, Duty the on-fraction, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SubDrive}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SubDrive}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sub-bass voice: a pure sine sub with a fast downward pitch envelope on the gate and a tanh drive for harmonic weight, the 808-style tuned sub-bass that sits under a mix. in0 = note, in1 = gate. Octave tunes it, PSweep the pitch-drop depth, Drive the saturation, Decay the amp tail, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SubDropVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SubDropVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sub-drop FX voice: the gate fires a deep sine whose pitch sinks slowly over the drop time from the note down into sub-bass, the cinematic 'sub drop' impact tail under a downbeat. in0 = note, in1 = gate. DropTime sets the fall length, Decay the amp tail, Drive the saturation, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SulPonticelloVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SulPonticelloVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sul-ponticello voice: bowing very close to the bridge, a thin glassy bowed tone unusually rich in high inharmonic-feeling overtones with a metallic edge, the scratchy ethereal ponticello colour. in0 = note, in1 = gate (bow). Octave tunes it, Vibrato the waver, Edge the harmonic bite, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SuperPad}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SuperPad}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Lush super-pad voice: a wide seven-saw unison plus a sub octave through a gentle resonant lowpass, the big, warm, slightly-detuned pad/chord bed. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Detune the unison width, Tone the lowpass brightness, Sub the sub-octave level, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Supersaw}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Supersaw}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV)}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

JP-8000 supersaw: seven detuned saws blended from centre to sides.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SyncStabVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SyncStabVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sync-stab voice: a hard-sync lead where a slave oscillator is reset by a master at the note pitch, struck as a short gated stab with a fast filter drop, the aggressive sync-stab of rave and big-room. in0 = note, in1 = gate. Octave tunes it, Sync the slave/master ratio, Decay the stab length, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SyncVCO}\blockicon{pedal}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SyncVCO}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonGreen}{\footnotesize[CIRCUIT]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM, Pitch Mod}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Analog-modeled hard-sync oscillator (the classic sync-sweep lead): a master oscillator at the note pitch resets a slave whose frequency is Sync times higher, so sweeping Sync (from an envelope or LFO into in1) produces the screaming formant sweep of oscillator hard sync. Octave tunes the master, Sync sets the slave/master ratio, Shape the slave waveform (saw->pulse), and Level the output. in1 = sync-sweep modulation (added to Sync), in2 = pitch mod. Naive reset (bright/aliased by design - that is the sync sound).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthAdditive}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthAdditive}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV)}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Additive oscillator: harmonic partials with rolloff and odd/even balance.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthFM}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthFM}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Carrier, Modulator}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Two-operator FM with a selectable modulator waveform (Wave: sine / triangle / saw / square / half-sine / abs-sine / squared / pulse) for richer-than-sine spectra, and an audio input on in 2 that acts as a third FM operator. Ratio tunes the modulator, Index sets FM depth, Octave the pitch, and Voices/Detune stack a detuned unison.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthGranular}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthGranular}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: —}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Granular oscillator: sprays overlapping grains from the sample slot. Pitch transposes each grain, Scan auto-advances the read position through the sample, and Dir plays grains in reverse.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthNoise}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthNoise}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: —}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Colored noise source (white/pink/brown).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthSub}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthSub}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV)}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sub oscillator: sine or square, octaves down, detuned.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthSync}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthSync}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV)}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Hard-sync oscillator: a master phasor resets a detuned slave saw.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthVector}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthVector}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV)}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Vector oscillator: XY morph across sine/saw/square/triangle.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthVoxVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthVoxVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Synth-vox voice: a synthetic choir pad, a bright saw stack pushed through a sweeping vowel bandpass that slowly morphs vowels, the wordless 'aaah-ooh' synth-choir of soundtrack pads. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod. Octave tunes it, VowelRate the morph speed, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TablaVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TablaVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tabla voice: the gate strikes a North-Indian tabla, the loaded head tuned so its membrane modes line up near-harmonically into a pitched ringing 'tin', a rare pitched drum. in0 = note (transposes), in1 = gate. Tune sets the pitch, Decay the sing, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TaikoVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TaikoVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Taiko voice: the gate strikes a large Japanese drum, a deep low membrane tone from inharmonic circular modes with a downward pitch bend and a noisy skin slap, thunderous and primal. in0 = note (transposes), in1 = gate. Tune sets the pitch, Decay the boom, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TalkingDrumVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TalkingDrumVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Talking-drum voice: a West-African hourglass pressure drum, a struck membrane whose pitch bends upward through the note (the squeezed tension-cords) for the speech-like glide of the talking drum. in0 = note, in1 = gate (strike). Octave tunes it, Bend the pitch glide, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TambourineVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TambourineVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tambourine voice: the gate shakes a tambourine, a burst of bright jingling metal zils (a cluster of high inharmonic squares) with a fast decay, the shimmery jingle of a hand tambourine. in1 = gate. Tone sets the jingle brightness, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TempleBlockVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TempleBlockVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Temple-block voice: a hollow wooden temple block (mokugyo), a struck dull woody resonance with a quick muffled decay and a faint hollow knock, the rounded percussive woodblock. in0 = note, in1 = gate (strike). Octave tunes it, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TenorSaxVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TenorSaxVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tenor-sax voice: a tenor saxophone, a fuller reedy sawtooth through a lower bandpass formant than the alto, with a breathy growl, a quick attack and vibrato, the warm tenor lead. in0 = note, in1 = gate (breath). Octave/Fine tune it, Vibrato the waver, Honk the formant bite, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TheatreOrganVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TheatreOrganVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Theatre-organ voice: a Wurlitzer cinema organ, lush tibia sine ranks thickened by a celeste detune and a wide tremulant, the silent-movie/skating-rink organ. in0 = note, in1 = gate. Octave tunes it, Celeste the rank detune, Tremulant the depth, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ThereminVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ThereminVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Theremin voice: the gesture-controlled classic, a pure heterodyne sine gliding between pitches with portamento and a gentle hand vibrato, the eerie sci-fi wail played without touch. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Glide the portamento, Vibrato the waver, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ThunderSheetVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ThunderSheetVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Thunder-sheet voice: the gate shakes a large metal sheet, a low rolling rumble of filtered noise under a shimmering metallic resonance that swells and slowly fades, the orchestral thunder-sheet effect. in1 = gate. Decay sets the roll length, Shimmer the metallic content, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TimbaleVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TimbaleVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Timbale voice: the gate strikes a shallow metal-shell drum, a bright tight membrane tone with a hard metallic rim ring, the cutting crack of Latin timbales. in0 = note (transposes), in1 = gate. Tune sets the head pitch, Rim the rim-ring amount, Decay the ring, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TinWhistleVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TinWhistleVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tin-whistle voice: an Irish penny whistle, a bright fipple-flute air-jet waveguide with a strong breathy chiff, the lively folk whistle of Celtic music. in0 = note, in1 = gate (breath). Octave/Fine tune it, Breath the air, Chiff the attack noise, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TomDrumVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TomDrumVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tom-drum voice: a pitched tom (the only fully note-tracked drum), a sine whose pitch drops from the played note with a resonant skin body and medium decay, the melodic tom fill. in0 = note, in1 = gate (trigger). Octave tunes it, Decay the body, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TomVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TomVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tom-drum voice: the gate strikes a mid-range sine with a gentle downward pitch bend and a medium decay, the round pitched thump of a tom. in0 = note (transposes), in1 = gate. Tune sets the pitch, Bend the drop depth, Decay the length, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TonewheelOrgan}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TonewheelOrgan}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tonewheel-organ voice: a Hammond-style additive organ summing nine drawbar harmonics (16',5 1/3',8',4',2 2/3',2',1 3/5',1 1/3',1') at preset registrations, with a percussion click on the gate and a slow rotary chorus. in0 = note, in1 = gate (percussion strike). Octave tunes it, Drawbars selects the registration, Percussion the click level, Rotary the chorus depth, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TonewheelVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TonewheelVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tonewheel voice: a vintage tonewheel organ with gentle harmonic leakage and a touch of motor hum between the wheels, the warm imperfect electromechanical organ. in0 = note, in1 = gate. Octave tunes it, Drawbars the brightness, Leakage the wheel cross-talk, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ToyPianoVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ToyPianoVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Toy-piano voice: a child's toy piano with small struck metal rods, a very short bright Karplus pluck with strongly inharmonic, bell-like overtones, the tinkly plinky toy sound. in0 = note, in1 = gate (strike). Octave/Fine tune it, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TR606HatVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TR606HatVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

606-hi-hat voice: the Roland TR-606 hi-hat, a thinner brighter metallic noise through a high bandpass with a tight decay, the trashy lo-fi hat of the 606. in1 = gate (trigger). Tone the brightness, Decay the length, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TR808HatVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TR808HatVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

808-hi-hat voice: the TR-808 hi-hat, a cluster of six detuned square oscillators through a high bandpass with a fast decay, the metallic tick of the 808. in1 = gate (trigger). Tone the brightness, Decay the open/closed length, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TR808KickVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TR808KickVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

808-kick voice: the booming Roland TR-808 bass drum, a sine whose pitch drops fast from a tuned start with a long amplitude decay and a click transient, the hip-hop sub-kick. in1 = gate (trigger). Tune the start pitch, Decay the boom length, Click the attack, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TR808SnareVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TR808SnareVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

808-snare voice: the TR-808 snare, two detuned tonal bodies plus a band of noise, decaying quickly, the snappy electro snare. in1 = gate (trigger). Tune the body pitch, Snappy the noise level, Decay the length, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TR909KickVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TR909KickVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

909-kick voice: the punchy Roland TR-909 bass drum, a sine with a faster, shorter pitch sweep and a prominent noisy click attack, the harder techno/house kick. in1 = gate (trigger). Tune the pitch, Decay the body, Punch the click, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TR909SnareVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TR909SnareVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

909-snare voice: the TR-909 snare, a tonal body under a heavier, brighter band of noise than the 808, the crisp sampled-flavoured house snare. in1 = gate (trigger). Tune the body, Snappy the noise, Decay the length, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TrautoniumVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TrautoniumVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Trautonium voice: the subharmonic synthesizer of the 1930s (used in Hitchcock's The Birds), a buzzy tone built from the undertone series - the fundamental plus pitches at 1/2, 1/3, 1/4 of it - for a hollow, organ-of-the-deep timbre. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Subs the undertone mix, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TremoloStringVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TremoloStringVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tremolo-string voice: a bowed string played with rapid back-and-forth bow strokes, a sawtooth body tone chopped by a fast amplitude tremolo, the shimmering suspense tremolo of film scores. in0 = note, in1 = gate (bow). Octave tunes it, TremRate the bow speed, Tone the cutoff, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TriangleVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TriangleVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Triangle (percussion) voice: the gate strikes a small steel triangle, a high bright cluster of inharmonic partials ringing with a long shimmering decay. in0 = note, in1 = gate. Pitch sets the bar size, Decay the ring, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TromboneVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TromboneVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Trombone voice: a tenor slide trombone, a broad sawtooth through a warmer lowpass than the trumpet with a slightly slower attack and gentle vibrato, the rich mid-brass. in0 = note, in1 = gate (breath). Octave/Fine tune it, Vibrato the waver, Bright the blare, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TromboneVoiceWG}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TromboneVoiceWG}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Trombone voice: a lip-reed bore with a long cylindrical-then-flared bore that glides smoothly between notes (the slide portamento), the bold brass of a trombone. in0 = note, in1 = gate (breath). Octave/Fine tune it, Breath the pressure, Glide the slide time, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TrumpetVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TrumpetVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Trumpet voice: a Bb trumpet, a bright buzzy sawtooth driven through a resonant lowpass whose cutoff opens with the breath so the tone blares brighter the harder it is blown, a quick lip attack and a light vibrato, the lead brass. in0 = note, in1 = gate (breath). Octave/Fine tune it, Vibrato the waver, Bright the blare, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TubaVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TubaVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tuba voice: the lowest brass, a deep sawtooth through a dark heavy lowpass with a slow breathy attack, the oom-pah foundation of the brass band. in0 = note, in1 = gate (breath). Octave/Fine tune it, Vibrato the waver, Dark the body, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TubaVoiceWG}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TubaVoiceWG}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tuba voice: a large lip-reed conical bore in the deep bass, self-oscillating on breath into the round fat foundation brass of a tuba. in0 = note, in1 = gate (breath). Octave/Fine tune it, Breath the pressure, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TubularBellVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TubularBellVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Tubular-bell voice: the gate strikes an orchestral chime tube, voiced from the tube's partials with the perceived strike pitch arising from the 4:5:6 cluster, ringing with a long majestic decay. in0 = note, in1 = gate. Octave tunes it, Decay the toll, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TwelveStringVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TwelveStringVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Twelve-string guitar voice: a Karplus pluck layered with a shimmering octave-course tap, giving the rich chorused jangle of a 12-string acoustic. in0 = note, in1 = gate (pluck). Octave/Fine tune it, Shimmer the octave-course level, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VCO}\blockicon{pedal}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VCO}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonGreen}{\footnotesize[CIRCUIT]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM, Pitch Mod}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Analog-modeled VCO (Moog/classic voltage-controlled oscillator character): a band-limited (PolyBLEP, anti-aliased) triangle->saw->pulse morph with slow analog Drift detuning the pitch and a gentle waveshape Warmth, for the fat, slightly-unstable vintage tone the clean base Osc does not model. Octave/Fine tune it, Shape sweeps triangle->saw->pulse, PWM sets the pulse width, Drift the analog pitch wander, Warmth a soft saturation, and Level the output. in1 = FM, in2 = pitch mod.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VeenaVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VeenaVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Veena voice: a South-Indian plucked lute, a Karplus pluck over a sustained sympathetic drone string, with a slight buzz from the wide bridge, the resonant raga voice of the veena. in0 = note, in1 = gate (pluck). Octave/Fine tune it, Drone the sympathetic-string level, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VibraphoneVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VibraphoneVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Vibraphone voice: the gate strikes an aluminium bar with near-octave partials and a long decay, amplitude-modulated by the spinning resonator-disc tremolo for the vibe's signature wobble. in0 = note, in1 = gate. Octave tunes it, Tremolo the motor speed, Decay the ring, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VibraslapVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VibraslapVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Vibraslap voice: the gate strikes a vibraslap, a sharp crack followed by the long rattling buzz of its loose metal pins chattering against the wooden box, the rattlesnake-buzz of the Latin percussion staple. in1 = gate. Decay sets the rattle length, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ViolaVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ViolaVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Viola voice: the alto bowed string, a sawtooth Helmholtz motion through a warmer mid body than the violin with a slow bow attack and vibrato, the mellow viola. in0 = note, in1 = gate (bow). Octave/Fine tune it, Vibrato the waver, Warmth the body, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ViolinVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ViolinVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Violin voice: a bowed string, a sawtooth approximating the Helmholtz motion through a bright body resonance with a slow bow attack and an expressive vibrato, the singing high violin. in0 = note, in1 = gate (bow). Octave/Fine tune it, Vibrato the waver, Bright the body, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ViolinVoiceWG}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ViolinVoiceWG}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Violin voice: a bowed-string waveguide self-oscillating into Helmholtz motion while the gate (bow) is held, with a singing vibrato, the orchestral violin as a playable voice. in0 = note, in1 = gate (bow). Octave/Fine tune it, BowForce the grip, Vibrato the waver, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VocoderVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VocoderVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM, Pitch Mod}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Vocoder voice: a 3-band channel vocoder that imposes the spectral envelope of the modulator signal (in2) onto a sawtooth carrier at the played note, so the carrier 'talks' with the dynamics of the modulator - classic robot-voice cross-synthesis. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod, in2 = modulator audio. Octave/Fine tune it, Carrier the saw/pulse mix, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Vosim}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Vosim}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM, Pitch Mod}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

VOSIM (VOcal SIMulation) oscillator: each fundamental period emits a burst of Pulses sin-squared pulses at FRatio times the pitch, each one Decay-scaled, then silence until the next period - the 1970s Kaegi-Tempelaars vocal/formant technique. FRatio places the formant region (vowel colour), Pulses sets the burst length and Decay the spectral tilt. Distinct from FOF granular and resonator formant synthesis; output is a unipolar pulse train.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VowelLeadVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VowelLeadVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM, Pitch Mod}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Vowel-morph lead voice: a sawtooth pushed through a sweepable formant bandpass that morphs across the A-E-I-O-U vowels, a mono talk-box lead with portamento glide. in0 = note, in1 = vowel sweep (added to Vowel), in2 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Vowel the formant position, Glide the portamento, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VoxContinentalVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VoxContinentalVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Vox Continental voice: the bright British combo organ, a hollow drawbar blend leaning on the fundamental and a strong octave with a reedy upper sparkle and fast gate, the 60s-beat organ. in0 = note, in1 = gate. Octave tunes it, Sparkle the upper drawbars, Vibrato the waver, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WalshVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WalshVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Walsh-function voice: the timbre is built by summing Walsh functions (the +/-1 square-wave orthogonal basis) instead of sines, giving a hard-edged blocky digital spectrum impossible with sine additive synthesis. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Order the number of Walsh terms (brightness), Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Wavefold}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Wavefold}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM, Pitch Mod}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

West-coast wavefolder voice: a sine pushed through a multi-stage triangle wavefolder (the Buchla/Serge timbre source) so raising Fold generates ever-denser harmonics by reflecting the wave off the rails, with Symmetry adding even harmonics. in0 = note, in1 = FM, in2 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Fold the folding gain, Symmetry the DC offset, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WaveshapeLead}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WaveshapeLead}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM, Pitch Mod}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Waveshaper lead: a sine driven through a Chebyshev-polynomial transfer curve so the Harmonics knob adds a precise number of harmonics (timbre by direct spectral control), the bright synthetic lead of waveshaping synthesis. in0 = note, in1 = FM, in2 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Harmonics the order, Drive the input gain, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WaveTerrainVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WaveTerrainVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Wave-terrain voice: the output is the height of a fixed 2-D surface z=f(x,y) read along an elliptical orbit whose angle advances at the note pitch, so changing the orbit radius sweeps the terrain and the timbre - wave-terrain synthesis. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod. Octave tunes it, Radius the orbit size, Warp the terrain shape, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WhipCrackVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WhipCrackVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Whip-crack voice: the gate fires a fast downward pitch zip ending in a sharp noise crack, the supersonic snap of a cracking whip. in1 = gate. TopHz sets the zip start, Crack the snap brightness, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WhistleVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WhistleVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Human-whistle voice: a person whistling, a nearly-pure sine with a soft breath-noise edge and an expressive vibrato that deepens on held notes, the airy melodic whistle. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Breath the air noise, Vibrato the waver, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WindChimeVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WindChimeVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Wind-chime voice: random gusts strike a set of tuned metal tubes, sounding soft pentatonic bell tones at random times with long shimmering decays, the gentle tinkle of a wind chime in a breeze. in0 = note (sets the chime key), in1 = unused. Activity sets how often the wind strikes, Decay the ring, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WindVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WindVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Breath/wind voice: white noise shaped by a resonant bandpass tracking the note pitch plus a slow gust LFO on amplitude, the airy whistling of wind through a tube or an overblown breath tone. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod. Octave/Fine tune it, Resonance the bandpass Q (airy vs tonal), Gust the amplitude wobble, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WobbleBassVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WobbleBassVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Wobble-bass voice (dubstep): a detuned-saw bass driven through a resonant lowpass whose cutoff is swept by an LFO at a musical rate, the rhythmic 'wub-wub' of dubstep. in0 = note, in1 = pitch mod. Octave tunes it, Wobble the LFO rate, Depth the sweep, Reso the filter bite, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WoodblockVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WoodblockVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Woodblock voice: the gate strikes a hollow wooden block, a dry pitched knock with a quick woody decay and a faint hollow second partial, the orchestral woodblock clop. in0 = note (transposes), in1 = gate. Tone sets the pitch, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Wt}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Wt}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM, Pitch Mod}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Wavetable oscillator: morphs a table bank with unison voices and Serum-style WARP (Bend/PWM/Sync/Mirror).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WurlitzerVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WurlitzerVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Wurlitzer voice: the reedy electric-piano tone of a Wurlitzer, a two-operator FM with a softer bark and a slight asymmetric grit distinct from the glassier Rhodes, struck on the gate with a medium decay. in0 = note, in1 = gate. Octave tunes it, Ratio the reed modulator, Bark the attack bite, Decay the length, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:XylophoneVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{XylophoneVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Xylophone voice: a hard-mallet-struck wooden bar, a bright additive tone with stretched inharmonic overtones and a short snappy decay, the percussive wooden xylophone. in0 = note, in1 = gate (strike). Octave tunes it, Decay the ring, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ZapLaser}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ZapLaser}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Zap/laser FX voice: the gate fires a sine whose pitch plummets from a high start in a fast exponential drop, the retro sci-fi laser/zap blip. in0 = note (sets the floor), in1 = gate. Top sets the start pitch, Drop the sweep speed, Decay the amp tail, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ZitherVoice}\blockicon{wave}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ZitherVoice}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentLight}{\footnotesize[OSC]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Pitch (CV), FM}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Zither voice: a multi-string plucked board that layers the plucked melody note with a sustained open drone string a fifth below, the autoharp/zither bed; the gate plucks the melody. in0 = note, in1 = gate (pluck). Octave/Fine tune it, Drone the open-string level, Level the output.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

## Pitch (1)

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthGlide}\blockicon{pitch}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthGlide}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[PIT]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Note (CV)}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Portamento: exponential glide of the note pitch.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

## Utility (4)

\phantomsection\label{blk:Scaler}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Scaler}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Signal}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

CV utility: scale, offset and bipolar curve-bend a control signal.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthGain}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthGain}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Gain trim in decibels.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SynthMixer}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SynthMixer}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: In A, In B}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Sums two inputs with independent levels.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VCA}\blockicon{utility}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VCA}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[UTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonMagenta}{\footnotesize[SYNTH]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio, Env}\quad\textcolor{neonCyan}{out: Audio}

Voltage-controlled amplifier: multiplies the signal by the control input.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

# MIDI (zpwr-midi-fx) (563 blocks)

## Control (5)

\phantomsection\label{blk:ATProc}\blockicon{knob}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ATProc}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[CTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Scales aftertouch, optionally converting it to a CC.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BendProc}\blockicon{knob}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BendProc}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[CTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Scales and/or inverts incoming pitch-bend.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CC2Note}\blockicon{knob}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CC2Note}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[CTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

A watched CC crossing a threshold triggers a note.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CCMap}\blockicon{knob}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CCMap}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[CTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Remaps a CC number and scales/offsets its value.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Note2CC}\blockicon{knob}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Note2CC}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[CTL]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Emits a CC from each note (pitch or velocity).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

## Dynamics (162)

\phantomsection\label{blk:AbundanceVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AbundanceVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Abundance velocity: accents each note by the signed abundance sigma(n)-2n, so deficient counts read soft, perfect counts sit at centre and abundant counts read loud; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AbundancyVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AbundancyVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Abundancy velocity: drives velocity from the abundancy index sigma(n)/n of the count - near 1 for primes, climbing past 2 for perfect and abundant numbers - so divisor-rich counts hit hardest; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Accent}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Accent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Boosts velocity on every Nth note.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AccentEveryN}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AccentEveryN}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Accent every N: boosts the velocity of every Nth note and slightly ducks the rest, stamping a steady metric downbeat onto an even stream - the simplest way to imply a time signature in the dynamics.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AccentOnHigh}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AccentOnHigh}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Accent on high: boosts the velocity of note-ons at or above a pitch threshold, brightening the top of the keyboard.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AccentOnLow}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AccentOnLow}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Accent on low: boosts the velocity of note-ons at or below a pitch threshold, reinforcing the bass register.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AccentPattern}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AccentPattern}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Accent pattern: boosts velocity on the on-steps of a 16-step accent bitmask and trims it elsewhere, stamping a repeating dynamic groove onto an even stream of notes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AliquotStepsVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AliquotStepsVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Aliquot-steps velocity: accents each note by how many steps the count's aliquot sequence (repeatedly summing proper divisors) takes before terminating or settling, an unpredictable number-theory accent; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AliquotVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AliquotVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Aliquot velocity: drives velocity from the aliquot sum (the sum of a number's proper divisors) of the note count, the quantity behind perfect, abundant and deficient numbers; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:AlternatingSumVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AlternatingSumVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Alternating-sum velocity: drives velocity from the alternating digit sum of the count (the signed digit total behind the divisible-by-11 test), an oscillating accent contour; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BackbeatAccent}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BackbeatAccent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Backbeat accent: boosts the velocity of notes on beats 2 and 4 (the backbeat) and slightly softens the others, stamping the rock/pop snare-accent feel onto a flat stream without dropping notes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BeattyVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BeattyVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Beatty velocity: drives velocity from the Beatty sequence floor(n*sqrt2) (an irrational-rotation staircase that, with its complement, partitions the integers); Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BernoulliSignVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BernoulliSignVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Bernoulli-sign velocity: alternates two velocity levels by the sign of the Bernoulli numbers (the alternating-sign even-index sequence from number theory and calculus), with a neutral level on the zero-valued odd indices.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BinaryRunVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BinaryRunVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Binary-run velocity: accents each note by the longest run of identical bits in its binary count, spiking on counts like 7, 15, 31 and 56; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BinaryWeightVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BinaryWeightVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Binary-weight velocity: drives velocity from the number of 1-bits (Hamming weight) in the note count, a self-similar accent pattern that pulses with the binary structure of the ordinal; Depth blends it with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CalkinWilfVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CalkinWilfVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Calkin-Wilf velocity: drives velocity from the Calkin-Wilf rational at the count (fusc(n)/fusc(n+1)), the breadth-first enumeration that lists every positive fraction exactly once; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CatalanVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CatalanVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Catalan velocity: steps velocity through the Catalan numbers (1,1,2,5,14,42,...) taken modulo a range, the combinatorial sequence counting balanced brackets and binary trees; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CollatzOddStepsVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CollatzOddStepsVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Collatz-odd-steps velocity: accents each note by how many odd (3n+1) rises its count takes on the way down the Collatz trajectory, an erratic hailstone-flavoured accent; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CollatzPeakVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CollatzPeakVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Collatz-peak velocity: accents each note by the log-height of the highest value its count reaches in the Collatz (3n+1) hailstone trajectory, so counts that soar before falling hit hardest; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CollatzVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CollatzVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Collatz velocity: maps each note's velocity to the number of 3n+1 steps its count takes to reach 1, an erratic deterministic accent sequence from the unsolved conjecture; Depth blends against the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CompositeRunVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CompositeRunVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Composite-run velocity: accents each note by how many composite counts have passed since the last prime, ramping up through prime gaps and snapping back to zero on each prime; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CototientVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CototientVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cototient velocity: accents each note by the cototient n minus Euler's totient (the count of integers up to n that share a factor with it), a divisor-flavoured accent complementary to the totient; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CrescendoLoop}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CrescendoLoop}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Crescendo loop: velocity climbs from Min to Max across Length notes then snaps back and repeats, an automatic sawtooth dynamic swell driven by the note count rather than the clock.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CrescendoRamp}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CrescendoRamp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Crescendo ramp: ramps note-on velocity up linearly across a phrase of N notes then resets, an automatic swell from soft to loud; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DigitalRootVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DigitalRootVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Digital-root velocity: accents each note by the digital root (the repeated digit-sum, 1..9) of its count, a perfectly periodic nine-step accent staircase from elementary number theory; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DigitEntropyVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DigitEntropyVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Digit-entropy velocity: drives velocity from how many distinct decimal digits the count uses, so repdigits read soft and varied counts read loud; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DigitMaxVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DigitMaxVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Digit-max velocity: drives velocity from the largest decimal digit of the note count, a coarse stepping accent that climbs and resets with the leading digits; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DigitMinVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DigitMinVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Digit-min velocity: drives velocity from the smallest decimal digit of the note count, the mirror of digit-max; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DigitProductVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DigitProductVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Digit-product velocity: accents each note by the product of its count's decimal digits, so counts with big digits hit hard and those with small digits stay soft; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DigitReverseVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DigitReverseVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Digit-reverse velocity: drives velocity from the count read backwards (123 becomes 321), an erratic accent that scrambles the steady climb of the counter into a jumpy pattern; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DigitRotateVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DigitRotateVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Digit-rotate velocity: rotates the count's decimal digits one place (the last digit jumps to the front) and reads velocity from the result, scrambling the steady counter into a jumpy pattern; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DigitSpreadVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DigitSpreadVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Digit-spread velocity: drives velocity from the spread between the largest and smallest decimal digit of the note count, so repdigit counts read soft and mixed-digit counts read loud; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DigitSumVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DigitSumVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Digit-sum velocity: derives velocity from the decimal digit-sum of the note count, a self-similar sawtooth-of-sawtooths accent that resets every power of ten; Depth blends it with the incoming velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DiminuendoRamp}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DiminuendoRamp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Diminuendo ramp: ramps note-on velocity down linearly across a phrase of N notes then resets, an automatic fade from loud to soft; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DistinctPrimeVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DistinctPrimeVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Distinct-prime velocity: accents each note by little-omega, the number of distinct prime factors of its count (primes softest, highly-composite counts loudest); Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DivisorCountVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DivisorCountVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Divisor-count velocity: makes notes louder on counts with many divisors, so highly-composite ordinals (12, 24, 36...) land as accents and primes stay quiet; Depth blends against the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DivisorProductVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DivisorProductVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Divisor-product velocity: drives velocity from the product of the note count's divisors (taken modulo a range), a number-theoretic accent that swings high for highly-composite counts; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DivisorSumVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DivisorSumVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Divisor-sum velocity: drives velocity from sigma(n), the sum of all divisors of the note count, so primes read low and highly-composite counts read loud; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:EuclideanAccentVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{EuclideanAccentVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Euclidean accent: boosts the velocity of notes landing on a Euclidean (Bjorklund) rhythm and softens the rest, stamping an evenly-distributed accent groove onto a steady stream of notes without dropping any.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:EuclideanVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{EuclideanVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Euclidean velocity: accents the notes that fall on the onsets of a Euclidean rhythm of Pulses-in-Steps and softens the rest, stamping a maximally-even world-rhythm groove onto the dynamics.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ExpDecayVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ExpDecayVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Exp-decay velocity: velocity starts loud and decays exponentially across a phrase of N notes then resets, an automatic decrescendo per phrase; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:FactorialBaseVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{FactorialBaseVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Factorial-base velocity: accents each note by the digit-sum of its count written in the factorial number system (where place values are 1!,2!,3!,...), an exotic mixed-radix accent contour; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:FactorialModVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{FactorialModVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Factorial-mod velocity: drives velocity from the count's factorial taken modulo 127, which collapses to zero past a small index (Wilson's theorem territory), giving a sharp early flurry then silence-velocity; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:FibVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{FibVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Fibonacci velocity: drives note velocity from the Fibonacci sequence taken modulo 128, climbing then wrapping in the golden cadence; Depth blends the pattern against the incoming velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GcdVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GcdVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

GCD velocity: sets velocity from the greatest common divisor of the note count and a modulus, so counts sharing big factors with the modulus hit harder - a quietly-periodic, factor-driven accent; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GhostNoteEveryN}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GhostNoteEveryN}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Ghost-note every N: softens every Nth note-on down to a quiet ghost-note level, dropping a recurring accent into the background for a syncopated groove.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}
