\phantomsection\label{blk:GhostNoteInject}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GhostNoteInject}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Ghost-note inject: randomly demotes a fraction of notes to a soft 'ghost' level, scattering the quiet in-between hits that give drum and bass parts their human, shuffling feel.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GoldbachCountVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GoldbachCountVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Goldbach-count velocity: accents each even count by the number of distinct ways it splits into a sum of two primes (its Goldbach partitions), a rising and jagged accent; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GoldenAngleVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GoldenAngleVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Golden-angle velocity: rotates each note count by the 137.5-degree golden angle (the phyllotaxis spiral that arranges sunflower seeds) and reads velocity from the resulting angle, an evenly-scattering accent; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GoldenSectionAccent}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GoldenSectionAccent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Golden-section accent: places a strong accent at the start and at the golden-ratio point (~0.618) of a repeating cycle, an aesthetically-balanced asymmetric stress pattern instead of an even backbeat.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GoldenVel}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GoldenVel}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Golden velocity: replaces each note's velocity with a value from the golden-ratio low-discrepancy sequence, which fills the dynamic range far more evenly than random, so accents never clump; Depth blends it against the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GrayCodeVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GrayCodeVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gray-code velocity: drives velocity from the reflected-binary Gray code of the note count (successive values differ by one bit), giving a smoothly-rotating, single-step-change accent pattern; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:HammingDistanceVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{HammingDistanceVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Hamming-distance velocity: drives velocity from how many bits flip between successive note counts (the Hamming distance of consecutive integers), a small spiky accent that jumps at binary carries; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:HappyVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{HappyVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Happy velocity: accents each note by how its count behaves under the happy-number process (sum of squared digits) - loud and bright if it reaches 1, darker if it falls into the sad cycle; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:HarmonicNumberVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{HarmonicNumberVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Harmonic-number velocity: drives velocity from the harmonic number H(n)=1+1/2+...+1/n, a smooth slowly-saturating logarithmic climb across the note count; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:HarmonicVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{HarmonicVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Harmonic velocity: shapes velocity along a 1/(k+1) harmonic-decay curve over a repeating Length-note cycle, so each phrase opens strong and tapers - an automatic accent-and-decay groove.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Humanize}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Humanize}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Adds random timing and velocity jitter for a human feel.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:HumanizeVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{HumanizeVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Humanize velocity: adds a small random variation to each note-on velocity so repeated hits no longer read as identical, a subtle natural-feel dither.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:IntervalVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{IntervalVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Interval velocity: sets each note's velocity from the size of its leap from the previous note, so wide jumps hit hard and stepwise motion stays gentle - dynamics that follow the melodic contour; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:JacobiVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{JacobiVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Jacobi-symbol velocity: picks one of three velocity levels from the Jacobi symbol of the count over an odd Modulus (+1, -1 or 0), a quadratic-residue signature from number theory; the modulus sets the pattern's period.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:JacobsthalVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{JacobsthalVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Jacobsthal velocity: drives velocity from the Jacobsthal numbers (J(n)=J(n-1)+2J(n-2): 1,1,3,5,11,21,...), a Fibonacci cousin tied to powers of two; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:KaprekarVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{KaprekarVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Kaprekar velocity: drives velocity from how many digit-sort-and-subtract steps the count takes to reach Kaprekar's constant 6174, an erratic 0-7 accent from a famous digit routine; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:KolakoskiVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{KolakoskiVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Kolakoski velocity: alternates two velocity levels following the self-describing Kolakoski sequence (whose run-lengths are the sequence itself), giving a hypnotically self-similar yet non-repeating accent pattern.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:LeonardoVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{LeonardoVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Leonardo velocity: drives velocity from the Leonardo numbers (L(n)=L(n-1)+L(n-2)+1: 1,1,3,5,9,15,25,...), the smoothsort sequence kin to Fibonacci; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:LogVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{LogVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Log velocity: drives velocity from the base-2 logarithm of the note count, a gentle ever-slowing ramp that doubles its reach each octave of counts; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:LookSayVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{LookSayVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Look-and-say velocity: drives velocity from the growing length of the look-and-say sequence (1, 11, 21, 1211, ...) at the note count, a self-describing sequence that lengthens by Conway's constant; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:LucasVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{LucasVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Lucas velocity: steps velocity through the Lucas numbers (the Fibonacci companion 2,1,3,4,7,11,...) taken modulo a range, a golden-cadence accent contour; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MarkovVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MarkovVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Markov velocity: walks the velocity through a near-neighbour Markov chain of levels, so dynamics drift coherently (mostly small changes, occasional leaps) rather than jumping randomly - a stochastic but musical accent contour.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MelodicAccent}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MelodicAccent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Melodic accent: accents notes that step up from the previous one and softens those that step down, so the dynamics follow the rise and fall of the melodic contour - phrasing that breathes with the line.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MobiusVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MobiusVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Moebius velocity: picks one of three velocity levels from the Moebius function mu of the count - high for squarefree-even-factor counts, low for squarefree-odd, neutral for counts with a squared factor - a number-theoretic three-state accent.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ModInverseVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ModInverseVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Mod-inverse velocity: drives velocity from the modular inverse of the note count under a chosen modulus (zero when the count shares a factor with the modulus), a number-theoretic scramble; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ModRangeVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ModRangeVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Mod-range velocity: ramps velocity in a repeating sawtooth across a chosen period of notes, a steady rising-then-resetting accent staircase; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MoebiusVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MoebiusVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Moebius velocity: accents notes by the Moebius function of the count - loud on squarefree numbers with an even number of prime factors, soft on odd, and mid-level on square-divisible ones; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:NarayanaVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{NarayanaVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Narayana velocity: drives velocity from Narayana's-cows sequence (a(n)=a(n-1)+a(n-3): 1,2,3,4,6,9,13,19,...), whose ratio tends to the supergolden ratio; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:NibbleSwapVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{NibbleSwapVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Nibble-swap velocity: scrambles velocity by swapping the high and low 4-bit nibbles of the note count, a byte-twiddling accent that jumps in a fixed but jagged pattern; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:OmegaVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{OmegaVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Omega velocity: sets velocity from the number of prime factors (with multiplicity) of the count, so smooth primes play soft and factor-heavy numbers play hard; Depth blends it with the incoming velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PadovanVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PadovanVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Padovan velocity: steps velocity through the Padovan sequence (P(n)=P(n-2)+P(n-3), the plastic-number recurrence) taken modulo a range, a slow, gently-rolling accent contour; Depth blends with the incoming velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PartitionVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PartitionVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Partition velocity: drives velocity from p(n), the number of ways to write the count as a sum of positive integers, a fast-growing combinatorial accent; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PellVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PellVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Pell velocity: drives velocity from the Pell numbers (P(n)=2P(n-1)+P(n-2): 1,2,5,12,29,70,...), the silver-ratio cousin of Fibonacci; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PerrinVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PerrinVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Perrin velocity: drives velocity from the Perrin sequence (P(n)=P(n-2)+P(n-3): 3,2,3,2,5,5,7,10,...), famous for its primality-test conjecture; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PersistenceVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PersistenceVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Persistence velocity: accents each note by the multiplicative-persistence depth of its count (how many digit-product steps reach a single digit), a sparse, spiky accent rooted in a famous open digit problem; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PitchClassVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PitchClassVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Pitch-class velocity: accents every note of a chosen pitch class and softens the rest, so a tonic or any scale degree rings out across the whole keyboard - a harmonic spotlight on one note name.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PopcountVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PopcountVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Popcount velocity: drives velocity from the number of 1-bits in the note count (its binary Hamming weight), a jagged bit-pattern accent; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PowerOfTwoFactorVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PowerOfTwoFactorVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Power-of-two-factor velocity: drives velocity from the largest power of two that divides the count, so odd counts read soft and highly-even counts read loud (a binary-divisibility accent); Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PrimeCountVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PrimeCountVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Prime-count velocity: drives velocity from the prime-counting function pi(count) - how many primes are at or below the note count - taken modulo a span, a slowly-climbing-then-wrapping accent rooted in the distribution of primes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PrimeFactorVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PrimeFactorVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Prime-factor velocity: sets velocity from the largest prime factor of the count (log-scaled), so prime counts ring loud and smooth, small-factored counts stay soft; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PrimeGapVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PrimeGapVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Prime-gap velocity: drives velocity from the size of the prime gap bracketing the note count (the distance between the primes just below and just above it), so notes near large prime deserts hit harder; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PrimeIndexAccent}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PrimeIndexAccent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Prime-index accent: accents the prime-numbered notes in the stream (the 2nd, 3rd, 5th, 7th, 11th, ... to pass) and softens the rest, an irregular number-theoretic accent pattern.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PrimeIndexVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PrimeIndexVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Prime-index velocity: drives velocity from the n-th prime number (the prime sitting at the running count's index), an irregularly-climbing accent straight from the prime sequence; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PrimePiVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PrimePiVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Prime-pi velocity: drives velocity from the prime-counting function pi(count) - how many primes are at or below the count - a slowly-climbing staircase accent from analytic number theory; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PrimeVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PrimeVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Prime velocity: drives velocity from the gaps between successive prime numbers, an irregular yet deterministic accent sequence that never settles into an obvious loop; Depth blends it with the incoming velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PrimorialModVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PrimorialModVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Primorial-mod velocity: drives velocity from the primorial (product of the first k primes) taken modulo 127, a fast-scrambling accent that jumps as each new prime multiplies in; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RadicalVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RadicalVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Radical velocity: accents each note by the radical of its count (the product of its distinct prime factors), so squarefree counts read high and prime-power counts read low; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Ramp}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Ramp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity ramp over a run of notes, ascending or descending.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RandomHoldVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RandomHoldVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Random-hold velocity: picks a random velocity and holds it for Hold notes before re-rolling, a sample-and-hold dynamic that gives blocky terraced changes rather than per-note jumps; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RegisterAccent}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RegisterAccent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Register accent: boosts the velocity of notes inside a Low..High pitch window and softens those outside, spotlighting one register's dynamics without dropping any notes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RepeatDecay}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RepeatDecay}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Repeat decay: each immediate repetition of the same note plays quieter by a decay factor, taming machine-gun retriggers into a natural fade and resetting the moment the pitch changes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ReverseBitsVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ReverseBitsVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Bit-reverse velocity: drives velocity from the 8-bit bit-reversal of the note count, scrambling the steady counter into the scattered order used by FFT bit-reversal; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ReverseSubtractVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ReverseSubtractVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Reverse-subtract velocity: drives velocity from the absolute difference between the count and its digit-reversal (the 196-algorithm / Kaprekar step), zero on palindromes and large on lopsided counts; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RoughnessVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RoughnessVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Roughness velocity: drives velocity from the smallest prime factor of the note count, the complement of smoothness, so even counts read low and prime-like counts read high; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RudinShapiroAccent}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RudinShapiroAccent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Rudin-Shapiro accent: pushes each note's velocity up or down by the +/-1 Rudin-Shapiro sequence of its count, an aperiodic accent pattern with a famously flat (white-like) spectrum - structured stress that never repeats predictably.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RulerVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RulerVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Ruler velocity: accents notes by the ruler sequence (the power of two dividing the count: 0,1,0,2,0,1,0,3,...), so every other note is a light tick and the downbeats of each binary level land harder - a self-similar metric accent.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RunningSumVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RunningSumVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Running-sum velocity: accumulates a small per-note increment into a velocity that drifts upward and wraps, a slowly-cycling ramp untied to pitch; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ScaleDegreeAccent}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ScaleDegreeAccent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Scale-degree accent: accents notes by their function in the key - tonic loudest, then fifth and third, with weak and chromatic degrees softened - carving a dynamic shape that follows the harmony.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ScaleHighlight}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ScaleHighlight}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Scale highlight: accents notes that belong to the chosen Root/Scale and softens the chromatic outsiders, spotlighting the key without removing any notes - a scale-aware dynamic emphasis.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SigmaVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SigmaVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Sigma velocity: drives velocity from sigma(count), the sum of all divisors of the note count, so highly-divisible counts hit harder - an accent contour straight out of number theory; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SineWaveVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SineWaveVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Sine-wave velocity: shapes velocity as a smooth sine across a chosen period of notes, a gentle rise-and-fall dynamic swell repeating every Period notes; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SmoothnessVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SmoothnessVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Smoothness velocity: drives velocity from the largest prime factor of the note count, so smooth (small-factor) counts read soft and prime counts read loud; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SquareDistanceVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SquareDistanceVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Square-distance velocity: accents notes by how close the count sits to a perfect square - loud right on the squares, fading in the gaps - a pulsing accent that widens as the squares spread apart; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SquareRootVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SquareRootVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Square-root velocity: drives velocity from the integer square root of the note count, a staircase whose steps grow ever wider; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SquareWaveVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SquareWaveVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Square-wave velocity: alternates every note between two fixed velocity levels, a hard 2-step on/off accent that drives a mechanical pumping groove.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:StaircaseVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{StaircaseVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Staircase velocity: velocity climbs in quantized steps up a staircase across a chosen number of steps then drops back, a terraced crescendo accent; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SternBrocotVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SternBrocotVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Stern-Brocot velocity: shapes velocity from Stern's diatomic (fusc) sequence, the fractal numerators of the Stern-Brocot tree of rationals, giving a self-similar sawtooth-of-sawtooths accent; Depth blends with the incoming velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SternDiatomicVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SternDiatomicVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Stern-diatomic velocity: accents each note by Stern's diatomic (fusc) sequence 1,1,2,1,3,2,3,1,4,... whose consecutive ratios enumerate every rational once (the Stern-Brocot tree); Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SwingVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SwingVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Swing velocity: accents on-beat notes and softens off-beat ones, applying the dynamic side of a swing/shuffle feel (the loud-soft alternation) independent of timing - instant groove from even input.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TernaryDigitVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TernaryDigitVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Ternary-digit velocity: drives velocity from the base-3 digit sum of the note count, a self-similar accent that climbs and resets on ternary carries; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ThueMorseTernaryVel}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ThueMorseTernaryVel}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Ternary Thue-Morse velocity: picks one of three velocity levels from the base-3 Thue-Morse sequence (digit-sum mod 3) of the count, a self-similar three-level accent that avoids short repeats; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ThueMorseVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ThueMorseVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Thue-Morse velocity: alternates two velocity levels by the cube-free Thue-Morse parity of the note count, accenting in a self-similar, never-quite-repeating pattern instead of a simple every-other.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TotientRatioVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TotientRatioVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Totient-ratio velocity: drives velocity from Euler's totient ratio phi(n)/n, so primes read near-full and highly-composite counts read soft, an arithmetic density accent; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TotientStepsVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TotientStepsVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Totient-depth velocity: accents each note by how many times Euler's totient must be applied to its count to reach 1 (the iterated-totient depth, roughly log-scaled); Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TotientVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TotientVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Totient velocity: drives velocity from Euler's totient ratio phi(n)/n of the count, dipping on numbers rich in small prime factors and peaking on primes - a number-theoretic accent contour; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TrailingOnesVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TrailingOnesVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Trailing-ones velocity: accents each note by how many 1-bits trail at the bottom of its binary count (0,1,0,2,0,1,0,3,...), a carry-driven spiky pattern; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TriangularModVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TriangularModVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Triangular-mod velocity: shapes velocity as a triangle wave rising and falling across a chosen period of notes, a smooth swell-and-fade accent contour; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TribonacciVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TribonacciVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Tribonacci velocity: drives velocity from the Tribonacci numbers where each term sums the previous three (0,1,1,2,4,7,13,24,44,...); Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TurnaroundAccent}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TurnaroundAccent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Turnaround accent: boosts the velocity of notes that are local melodic turning points - the peaks and valleys where the line changes direction - emphasising the contour's corners.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:UnitaryDivisorVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{UnitaryDivisorVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Unitary-divisor velocity: accents each note by the sum of its count's unitary divisors (divisors that share no factor with their cofactor), a coprime-divisor variant of the sigma accent; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelByPitch}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelByPitch}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity by pitch: sets each note's velocity from its keyboard position (a Slope per semitone around middle C plus a Base), so high notes can ring louder or softer - keyboard-position dynamics.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelClip}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelClip}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Clamps velocity between Min and Max.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelCompress}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelCompress}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity compressor: velocities above Threshold are pulled toward it by Ratio (1 = off, 8 = hard), narrowing the dynamic range smoothly instead of hard-clamping like VelClip.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelCurve}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelCurve}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Reshapes velocity through a power curve.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelDrift}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelDrift}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity drift: each note's velocity takes one step of a bounded random walk, so dynamics wander smoothly and correlatedly over time instead of jumping independently like Humanize - the slow swell and ebb of a tiring player.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelGate}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity gate: drops any note whose velocity falls outside the Min..Max window, so only soft (or only hard) hits pass - a dynamics-dependent filter for splitting ghosted from accented playing.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelGateHysteresis}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelGateHysteresis}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity Schmitt gate: opens once a note exceeds the High velocity and stays open until one drops below Low, so soft passages between accents either all pass or all mute - hysteretic dynamic gating that avoids chattering at the threshold.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelInvert}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelInvert}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Flips the velocity scale around its midpoint (soft <-> loud) by Amount, so your accents become ghost notes and the ghosts become accents - a velocity mirror, blendable to taste.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelKey}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelKey}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Keyboard velocity tracking: scales each note's velocity by how far its pitch sits from a Center note, Tilt setting how strongly and which way - higher notes louder (or softer), the dynamic key-tracking of real instruments.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelLength}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelLength}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Note duration scales with velocity: hard notes ring for Max ms, soft notes for Min (set Max < Min to make accents staccato) - the played articulation where dynamics shape note length.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Velocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Velocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Scales and offsets note velocity, with a modulation amount.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityAlternate}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityAlternate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity alternate: alternates note-on velocities between two fixed levels (loud, soft, loud, soft), an automatic backbeat-style dynamic groove.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityBoost}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityBoost}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity boost: adds a fixed amount (positive or negative) to every note-on velocity, clamped to the legal range, a quick dynamics offset.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityClip}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityClip}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity clip: hard-limits note-on velocities to a floor and ceiling, clamping anything outside the window to the edges (a dynamic gate/limiter).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityCompress}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityCompress}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity compress: pulls every note-on velocity toward the centre by the set ratio, narrowing the dynamic range so soft and loud notes sit closer together.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityCurve}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityCurve}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity curve: reshapes note-on velocity through a gamma curve - values above 1 soften the response (more playing in the quiet range), below 1 harden it.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityDrift}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityDrift}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity drift: applies a slow bounded random walk to note-on velocities so the dynamics wander gently up and down over time rather than jumping; Step sets the walk speed.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityExpand}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityExpand}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity expand: pushes velocities away from the mid-level (the opposite of compression), so soft notes get softer and loud notes louder - widening the dynamic range of a flatly-played or over-quantized part.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityFloor}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityFloor}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity floor: lifts any note played softer than the floor up to it, guaranteeing every note speaks at a minimum level - a reverse noise gate for taming ghost notes and uneven controllers.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityFold}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityFold}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity fold: folds note-on velocities that exceed a ceiling back downward (a wavefolder for dynamics), so very hard hits read softer in a non-monotonic way.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityFromInterval}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityFromInterval}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity from interval: accents each note by how far it leaps from the previous note, so large melodic jumps hit harder than stepwise motion; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityFromPitch}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityFromPitch}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity from pitch: derives velocity from the note's pitch so higher notes play louder (or, inverted, lower notes louder), a keyboard-tilt dynamic; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityGammaCurve}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityGammaCurve}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity gamma curve: reshapes velocity through a gamma transfer curve - values below 1 lift soft notes and compress dynamics, above 1 push them down and expand - a smooth nonlinear dynamics bend.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityGate}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity gate: passes only notes whose velocity falls inside a Low..High window, masking soft or loud notes (a dynamics filter / range splitter); Invert mutes the window instead of keeping it.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityLFO}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityLFO}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity LFO: sweeps velocity up and down with a slow sine over a set number of notes, an automatic dynamic swell-and-fade that breathes life into a flat sequence; Depth sets how deep the swell.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityMultiply}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityMultiply}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity multiply: scales every note-on velocity by a fixed gain factor, a simple dynamics trim that boosts or attenuates the whole part.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityQuantize}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityQuantize}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity quantize: snaps note-on velocities to a small number of evenly-spaced levels, a terraced/stepped dynamic reminiscent of early samplers and harpsichords.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityRandomScale}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityRandomScale}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity random scale: multiplies each note's velocity by an independent random gain, scattering the dynamics for a more human, less machine-perfect feel (timing untouched, unlike Humanize); Amount sets the scatter width.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityRandomWalk}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityRandomWalk}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity random walk: nudges velocity by a smoothed, bounded random walk so the dynamics drift and breathe over time rather than jumping independently each note, a gradual humanizing of touch.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocitySmooth}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocitySmooth}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity smooth: glides each note's velocity toward a running average of the recent ones, ironing out wild dynamic jumps into an even, controlled performance; Amount sets how strongly it pulls toward the average.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocitySwap}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocitySwap}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity swap: inverts each note-on velocity (128 minus the value) so the softest hits become the loudest and vice versa; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelStep}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelStep}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity step: quantizes velocity to a small number of levels (a dynamics bit-crush), flattening expressive playing into stepped terraces from gentle 8-level to brutal 2-level.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WythoffVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WythoffVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Wythoff velocity: drives velocity from the lower Wythoff sequence floor(n*phi) (the golden Beatty sequence, the winning positions of Wythoff's game: 1,3,4,6,8,9,11,...); Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ZeckendorfVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ZeckendorfVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Zeckendorf velocity: accents each note by how many Fibonacci numbers it takes to sum to the note count (its Zeckendorf representation length), a number-theoretic accent that grows in golden steps; Depth blends with the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ZipfVelocity}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ZipfVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Zipf velocity: draws each note's velocity from a Zipf (1/rank) distribution, so a few dynamic levels dominate and loud accents are rare - the power-law dynamics of natural performance; Depth blends it against the played velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ZoneVelocityScale}\blockicon{dynamics}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ZoneVelocityScale}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[DYN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Zone velocity scale: scales the velocity of notes inside [Low,High] by Gain, a per-zone dynamics trim that lets a split layer sit louder or softer than the rest.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

## Expression (3)

\phantomsection\label{blk:Fall}\blockicon{block}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Fall}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Jazz fall-off: on release the pitch sweeps down by Depth over Time while the note rings, then the note releases and the bend snaps back to centre. The note-off is delayed to the end of the fall.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiVibrato}\blockicon{block}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiVibrato}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

A pitch-bend LFO that fades in after an Onset delay while any note is held - the delayed, played-in vibrato of a string or voice rather than an instant wobble. Rate in Hz, Depth as bend amount.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Scoop}\blockicon{block}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Scoop}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Bends up into each struck note from Depth below, resolving to pitch over Time - the vocal/brass scoop, a pitch-bend ramp that ends at centre so no bend is left hanging.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

## Harmony (51)

\phantomsection\label{blk:AutoInvert}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{AutoInvert}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cycles the inversion of each struck chord: every trigger rotates which of the lowest notes jump an octave (by Rotate), so a repeated chord keeps climbing through its inversions - movement ChordVoicing's static shapes don't give.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Bassline}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Bassline}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Follows the chord with a mono bass voice: the lowest held note dropped Octaves down, retriggered whenever the lowest note changes. Passes the original notes through and adds the bass beneath them.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Chord}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Chord}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Turns each incoming note into a chord (165 types) with inversion, spread, octave doubling and strum.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ChordLock}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ChordLock}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Quantizes every note to the nearest tone of a fixed chord (Root + Type), tighter than Scale's scale-quantize - locks an improvisation to a chord's notes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ChordMem}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ChordMem}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Each key plays a stored chord shape (semitone intervals from node config).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ChordProg}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ChordProg}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Each note triggers the next chord in a stored root progression (node config).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ChordVoicing}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ChordVoicing}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Re-voices each note into a jazz shape (open / shell / quartal / spread).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ConsonanceGate}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ConsonanceGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Consonance gate: passes a note only when its interval from the previous one is consonant (unison, third, fourth, fifth, sixth or octave), filtering a chromatic line down to smooth, dissonance-free melodic motion.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ContourGate}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ContourGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Contour gate: passes only the rising moves (or only the falling moves) of a melody relative to the previous kept note, isolating the ascending or descending gestures of a line.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ContourReverseGate}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ContourReverseGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Contour-reverse gate: passes a note only when the melody changes direction (a peak or valley), keeping just the turning points of a line and dropping the runs between them; Invert keeps the runs.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Counterpoint}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Counterpoint}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Adds a second voice in contrary motion - when the melody rises the counter voice falls and vice-versa - quantised to Root/Scale. Interval sets the starting separation.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Diatonic}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Diatonic}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Transposes by scale DEGREES within Root/Scale (a third, a fifth...) instead of by raw semitones like Transpose - the shift always lands back in key. Mod Amt adds a modulatable degree offset.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DiatonicChord}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DiatonicChord}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Auto-harmoniser: builds the in-key chord on every note by stacking diatonic thirds (triad, 7th...) along Root/Scale, so single-finger melodies become correct chords in the key.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DiatonicHarmonize}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DiatonicHarmonize}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Diatonic harmonize: adds a second voice a chosen number of scale steps above each note within a major key, so the harmony note always stays in key (true diatonic thirds/sixths, unlike a fixed chromatic interval).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DiatonicTranspose}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DiatonicTranspose}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Diatonic transpose: shifts every note by a number of whole scale degrees within a major key, so the melody moves up or down the staff while always landing on in-key notes - true in-key transposition, unlike the chromatic kind.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Drone}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Drone}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Doubles every note with a fixed pedal root.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GhostNote}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GhostNote}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Ghost note: doubles every note with a quiet copy a set interval below (default an octave), at a fraction of the original velocity - the subliminal low reinforcement drummers and bassists use to thicken a line.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Glissando}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Glissando}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Fills a stepwise run between consecutive notes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GravityQuantize}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GravityQuantize}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gravity quantize: pulls every note toward a center pitch by an adjustable strength, as if the keyboard had a gravitational well - gentle settings nudge stray notes inward, strong settings collapse a whole part toward one tone.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:HarmonicQuantize}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{HarmonicQuantize}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Harmonic quantize: re-pitches each note to the nearest member of the natural harmonic series above a root, snapping a chromatic part onto the pure, stretched-then-compressed intervals of the overtone series.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Harmonize}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Harmonize}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Adds up to three fixed-interval harmony voices to each note.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:IntervalClassGate}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{IntervalClassGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Interval-class gate: passes a note only when its leap from the previous kept note belongs to a chosen interval class (0 unison/octave .. 6 tritone), filtering a line down to one harmonic colour of motion; Invert keeps the rest.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Invert}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Invert}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Mirrors pitch around a pivot note.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:LeapGate}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{LeapGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Leap gate: passes only stepwise motion (intervals below the threshold) or only leaps (intervals at or above it) relative to the previous kept note, splitting a melody into its smooth and its jagged motion.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiCluster}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiCluster}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Adds symmetric tone-cluster voices above and below each note.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiFold}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiFold}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Folds out-of-range notes back inside a Low..High window.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MirrorPivot}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MirrorPivot}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Mirror pivot: reflects every note about a fixed pivot pitch (out = 2*Pivot - note), turning rising lines into falling ones and inverting every interval around the chosen axis - melodic inversion with a movable mirror.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ModalShift}\blockicon{harmony}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ModalShift}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[HRM]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}
