\phantomsection\label{blk:NoteToGateCC}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{NoteToGateCC}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Note-to-gate CC: emits a chosen controller high while any note is on and low when it releases, turning note events into a gate/trigger control signal for envelopes, side-chains or external gear.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:NoteToPitchBend}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{NoteToPitchBend}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Note-to-pitch-bend: emits a pitch-bend proportional to each note's distance from a center pitch, so notes far from the center are pre-bent - a way to map keyboard position onto continuous detune or to drive a synth's bend input from played pitch.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PanByPitch}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PanByPitch}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Pan by pitch: emits a pan CC (controller 10) that places low notes to the left and high notes to the right, an automatic keyboard-position stereo spread as a synth or sampler plays.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PolyChan}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PolyChan}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Round-robins each new note to the next of Voices MIDI channels from Base, spreading a polyphonic part across a multitimbral rig - one note per channel, the classic voice-allocation split (distinct from MPEConvert's per-note MPE channels).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RandomPanCC}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RandomPanCC}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Random-pan CC: fires a random pan (controller 10) value on every note-on, scattering successive notes across the stereo field for an automatic, ever-shifting spatial spread; Spread sets how wide the scatter.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Split}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Split}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Keyboard split: notes below the split point transpose by Low, the rest by High.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SplitTranspose}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SplitTranspose}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Split transpose: a keyboard split where notes at or above the Split point are transposed by Semitones and notes below pass untouched, so two hands play in different registers.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Thin}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Thin}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Note-density limiter: drops any note-on arriving within MinGap ms of the last one passed, decluttering a busy stream or fast repeats - distinct from NoteLimit, which caps simultaneous polyphony. A dropped note's off is dropped too.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Transformer}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Transformer}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Rule: notes within a range are transposed and velocity-scaled.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelocityZoneGate}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelocityZoneGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Velocity-zone gate: passes only notes whose velocity falls inside [Min,Max] and drops the rest; Invert keeps only the notes outside the velocity window.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:WrapAround}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{WrapAround}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Wrap around: notes that rise above the High edge wrap back to the Low end of the range (and vice versa), a modular pitch fold that keeps a part inside a register while letting runs cycle around.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ZoneOctaveShift}\blockicon{routing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ZoneOctaveShift}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[RTE]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Zone octave shift: shifts the notes inside [Low,High] by whole Octaves, leaving notes outside the zone where they are, a register jump confined to one key range.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

## Sequencing (63)

\phantomsection\label{blk:Appoggiatura}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Appoggiatura}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Leaning grace note: each struck note first sounds a long auxiliary (Interval away) that takes Time from the principal, which then sustains - the expressive long grace, unlike the quick Flam or Mordent.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Arp}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Arp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Arpeggiator: cycles held notes through a pattern at a synced rate with gate, octaves, swing and step count. Latch holds the chord so it keeps arpeggiating after the keys are released (the next key starts a new chord).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BeattyGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BeattyGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Beatty gate: passes note-ons on a Beatty sequence floor(n*Ratio) for any irrational ratio, a Sturmian, maximally-even thinning whose density is set by the ratio; Invert keeps the complement.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:BurstGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{BurstGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Burst gate: lets OnLen consecutive notes through, mutes the next OffLen, and repeats, chopping a steady run into rhythmic bursts and rests - a note-count gate for stutter and ratchet feels.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Cascade}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Cascade}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Each struck note fans out into a timed run of Steps notes climbing (or falling) the scale from it - an arpeggiated flourish fired per key, unlike Arp which cycles the whole held chord.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ChordArp}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ChordArp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cthulhu-style chord arpeggiator: each key builds a full chord (Type) and the result is arpeggiated upward across Octaves at a synced Rate - one finger plays a moving arpeggio of the whole chord. Releasing the key stops its run.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ClaveGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ClaveGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Clave gate: passes notes on the pulses of the Afro-Cuban son clave, the five-stroke rhythmic key of salsa and Latin music, selectable as the 3-2 or 2-3 orientation; Invert keeps the off-clave notes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:ClockDivideGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{ClockDivideGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Clock divider: passes one note-on out of every Divide that arrive and drops the rest, a simple note-rate divider for thinning fast passages or extracting a downbeat pulse.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CongruentGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CongruentGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Congruent gate: passes a note only when its running count is congruent to Remainder modulo Modulus, a fully-parameterized periodic gate that covers every-Nth-note and offbeat patterns from two numbers; Invert keeps the rest.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:CrossPulseGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{CrossPulseGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cross-pulse gate: lays two pulse grids of different periods over the note stream and passes notes where they coincide (AND) or where either fires (OR), generating polyrhythmic and cross-rhythmic gating from two simple counts.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Drunk}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Drunk}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Random-walk melodic sequencer: each synced Rate step nudges the pitch by a random +/-Step semitones, bounded within +/-Range of the lowest held note - Brownian melody anchored to the chord, unlike MidiRandom's memoryless picks.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:DurationFromPitch}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{DurationFromPitch}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Duration from pitch: maps each note's pitch to its length so high notes play short and low notes long (or, inverted, the reverse), a register-tilt articulation.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Echo}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Echo}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

MIDI delay: repeats notes at a synced time with velocity feedback decay.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:EuclidComplementGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{EuclidComplementGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Euclidean complement: passes notes that fall on the rests of a Euclidean rhythm of Pulses-in-Steps, the photo-negative of a Euclid sequencer - an instant interlocking counter-rhythm.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:EuclideanGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{EuclideanGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Euclidean gate: passes notes on the pulses of a Euclidean (Bjorklund) rhythm that spreads Pulses as evenly as possible over Steps - the algorithm that reproduces clave, tresillo and most world rhythms from two numbers; Rotation shifts the pattern. Invert keeps the rest.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:EuclidMel}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{EuclidMel}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Euclidean melody: spreads Pulses evenly across Steps (Bjorklund) and, on each hit, advances a cursor through the held chord - the rhythm is Euclidean and the pitch walks the chord, unlike SeqEuclid which stabs all held notes per pulse.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Flam}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Flam}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Precedes every note with a quiet grace note, delaying the main hit so the grace leads it - the classic drum flam.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GateArp}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GateArp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cthulhu's arp gate lane: walks the held chord upward across Octaves while the note length cycles a per-step staccato/legato pattern, so the rhythm breathes - short stabs then sustained notes. Gate scales the pattern (>1 overlaps into the next step).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GateLengthAccent}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GateLengthAccent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gate-length accent: holds every Nth note (the downbeat) for the Long duration and clips the rest to Short, a rhythmic gate accent.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GateLengthAlternate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GateLengthAlternate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gate-length alternate: alternates note durations between two lengths (long, short, long, short), a bouncing gate groove.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GateLengthFromInterval}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GateLengthFromInterval}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gate-length from interval: grows each note's duration with how far it leaps from the previous note, so big melodic jumps ring longer than stepwise motion.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GateLengthInvVelocity}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GateLengthInvVelocity}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gate-length inverse velocity: louder notes are clipped short and soft notes ring long (the inverse of VelLength), a punch-then-decay articulation.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GateLengthLFO}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GateLengthLFO}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gate-length LFO: sweeps note duration between Min and Max with a sine that completes one cycle every Period notes, a breathing gate that swells and shrinks.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GateLengthRamp}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GateLengthRamp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gate-length ramp: ramps note duration linearly from Min to Max across Steps notes then resets, an automatic gate crescendo.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GateLengthRandom}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GateLengthRandom}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gate-length random: gives each note a random duration between Min and Max, humanising the gate so no two notes ring exactly the same length.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:GoldenWordGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{GoldenWordGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Golden-word gate: gates notes by the infinite Fibonacci word (the Sturmian 0/1 sequence at golden-ratio spacing), the most-ordered aperiodic rhythm there is; Invert flips which letter plays.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:KolakoskiGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{KolakoskiGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Kolakoski gate: gates notes by the self-describing Kolakoski 1,2 sequence (whose run-lengths reproduce the sequence itself), a hypnotically self-similar aperiodic pattern; Invert flips which symbol plays.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiBrianBrain}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiBrianBrain}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Brian's Brain MIDI sequencer: an 8x8 three-state grid (ready / firing / dying) evolves under Brian's Brain rules - a ready cell ignites only with exactly two firing neighbours, firing cells pass to dying, dying cells reset. Each clock step plays a note for every firing cell in the next column (row maps to a scale degree from Root/Scale); a full 8-step sweep advances one generation. The refractory state keeps it restless and sparkly where Game of Life settles. Density sets the random fill. The MIDI-note counterpart to the BrianBrain CV block.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiGameOfLife}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiGameOfLife}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Conway's Game of Life MIDI sequencer: an 8x8 cell grid evolves under the B3/S23 rule; each clock step reads the next column and plays a note for every live cell (row maps to a scale degree from Root/Scale), and a full 8-step sweep advances one generation. Gliders, blinkers and still-lifes become ever-shifting melodic and harmonic patterns. Density sets the random fill when the grid (re)seeds. The MIDI-note counterpart to the GameOfLife CV block.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiLangton}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiLangton}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Langton's Ant MIDI melody: a single turmite walks an 8x8 grid - turning right on a white cell, left on a black one, flipping each cell as it leaves, then stepping forward. Each clock advances the ant Steps cells and plays one note for its current row (mapped to a scale degree from Root/Scale), so the melody scribbles chaotically then locks into the famous periodic 'highway'. Emergent order from one trivial rule - a single moving agent, not a population.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiMarkov}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiMarkov}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Learns the note-to-note transition statistics of what you play (a 12x12 pitch-class chain) and, on each synced step, walks the chain to improvise a new line in the same style. Memory fades old transitions.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiQuantize}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiQuantize}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Snaps note starts to a synced grid by a strength amount.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiTranceGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiTranceGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Chops the held notes with a 16-step on/off Pattern at a synced Rate: every 'on' step retriggers all held notes, gated to a fraction of the step. The rhythmic gate behind the trance sound - pattern-driven, where Roll is a fixed pulse.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiTuring}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiTuring}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Turing-machine sequencer (Music Thing style): a circular shift register clocks once per step and the held notes fire when the read bit is 1. Change is the probability the recycled bit flips - 0 locks an evolving loop, 1 fully randomises, between = slowly mutating melodies.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Mordent}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Mordent}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

One-shot ornament: each struck note plays principal -> auxiliary -> principal at the attack, then sustains, the single-flick counterpart to the continuous Trill.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:NoteLength}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{NoteLength}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Forces a fixed note duration (gate length).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:NoteOffDelay}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{NoteOffDelay}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Extends each note's gate by delaying its note-off.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:OctaveArp}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{OctaveArp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cthulhu's arp octave lane: arpeggiates the held chord while a per-step octave-offset Pattern leaps the line up and down by whole octaves (alternating, staircase, bounce...) instead of climbing monotonically like StepArp.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PaperfoldGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PaperfoldGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Paperfold gate: gates notes by the regular paperfolding (dragon-curve) sequence of the count, a 2-automatic pattern that folds in on itself at every scale; Invert flips the kept and dropped halves.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PatternGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PatternGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gates note-ons through a 16-step on/off pattern (bitmask).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Polymeter}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Polymeter}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Two step lanes of different lengths (LenA, LenB) clock together over the held notes, phasing against each other so the combined pattern only repeats after lcm(LenA,LenB) steps - instant polymetric movement from one chord.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:PolyrhythmGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{PolyrhythmGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Polyrhythm gate: passes notes that land on a multiple of either count A or count B, overlaying two pulse grids (e.g. 3 against 4) into an interlocking cross-rhythm of accents.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:RatchetArp}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{RatchetArp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cthulhu's arp repeat lane: walks the held chord upward across Octaves while a per-step pattern retriggers selected steps multiple times (capped by Max), packing rolls into the line. Unlike SeqRatchet's fixed count, the repeat count varies per step.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Ricochet}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Ricochet}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Accelerating bouncing retriggers with decaying velocity.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Roll}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Roll}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Drum roll / buzz: retriggers every held note at Rate for as long as it is held, each hit gated to a fraction of the interval.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SeqEuclid}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SeqEuclid}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Euclidean rhythm gate: spreads Pulses evenly across Steps (rotatable) at a synced rate.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SeqRatchet}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SeqRatchet}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Retriggers each note Count times within a synced step.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Shift}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Shift}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Delays every note by Delay ms (groove nudge / lay-back), pushing note-ons and offs back by the same amount so durations are preserved - the per-track timing offset of a groove sequencer.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SkipArp}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SkipArp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cthulhu's arp skip lane: arpeggiates the held chord upward across Octaves, but each step has a Skip probability of being dropped to a rest, thinning the pattern differently every cycle for generative movement.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:SkipNote}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{SkipNote}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Skip note: drops every Nth note-on and lets the rest through, the inverse of a clock divider - a quick way to punch regular holes in a run of notes.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Spiral}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Spiral}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Transposing MIDI delay: each repeat is delayed by Time and shifted by Interval semitones with velocity decaying by Feedback, so one note spins off a climbing or falling staircase of echoes - unlike Echo, which repeats the same pitch.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:StaccatoCut}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{StaccatoCut}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Staccato cut: clips every note to a short fixed length for a crisp detached articulation, no matter how long the key was held.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:StepArp}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{StepArp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cthulhu-style step arpeggiator: walks the held chord strictly upward across Octaves octaves at a synced Rate, accenting the first step of each cycle - the rolling, octave-jumping arp of Xfer Cthulhu's sequencer. Distinct from Arp's mode-cycling: this climbs the chord in order.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:StepSeqMidi}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{StepSeqMidi}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Melodic step sequencer - semitone offsets (node config) clock the held root.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Strum}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Strum}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Spreads a chord's notes over time, up or down, like a guitar strum.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Swing}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Swing}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Pushes off-beat eighth-notes later for a swing/shuffle feel.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TenutoHold}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TenutoHold}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Tenuto hold: extends every note to a long fixed length so notes sustain and run into each other, a held legato-style gate.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TransArp}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TransArp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cthulhu's arp transpose lane: arpeggiates the held chord upward across Octaves while a per-step semitone Pattern (fifth pedals, triad arps, scalar runs) is added on top, so the line outlines a melodic contour, not just the chord tones.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TresilloGate}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TresilloGate}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Tresillo gate: passes notes on the tresillo 3-3-2 pattern, the eight-step rhythmic cell underlying reggaeton, habanera and countless Latin grooves; Invert keeps the rest.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Trill}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Trill}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Keyboard trill: while a note is held, alternates rapidly between it and the note Interval semitones above, at Rate.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Tuplet}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Tuplet}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Snaps note starts to a Tuplets-per-beat grid (3 = triplets, 5 = quintuplets) by Strength, for a triplet or odd-tuplet feel where MidiQuantize only snaps to the straight grid.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Turn}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Turn}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Gruppetto turn: a four-step ornament at the attack - upper auxiliary, principal, lower auxiliary, then the principal held. Time sets each step, Interval the neighbour distance.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:VelArp}\blockicon{sequence}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{VelArp}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[SEQ]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonCyan}{\footnotesize[CV]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cthulhu's arp velocity lane as a ramp: arpeggiates the held chord while velocity sweeps from From to To across Steps then resets, so every pass swells or fades without a per-step editor.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

## Timing (2)

\phantomsection\label{blk:HumanizeTiming}\blockicon{block}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{HumanizeTiming}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Humanize timing: nudges each event's timing by a small random amount (up to the set sample range) so a rigid sequenced part feels played by hand.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:StrumChord}\blockicon{block}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{StrumChord}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[FX]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Strum chord: staggers the start of simultaneously-played notes by a small delay so a chord is strummed across the strings rather than struck as a block, with selectable up- or down-stroke direction; Spread sets the strum speed.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

## Tuning (3)

\phantomsection\label{blk:JustTune}\blockicon{tuning}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{JustTune}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[TUN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Adaptive just intonation: retunes each note toward its 5-limit JI ratio relative to Key via per-note detune (polyphonic), Amount blending from equal temperament to pure - the beatless thirds and fifths of real harmony.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Microtuner}\blockicon{tuning}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Microtuner}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[TUN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Stretched-octave microtuning via per-note pitch detune.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:TuneTable}\blockicon{tuning}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{TuneTable}}\thinspace\textcolor{textDim}{\footnotesize[TUN]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Custom microtuning: detunes each note by a per-pitch-class cents offset from the node's 12-value config list (relative to Key) - meantone, Pythagorean, maqam or gamelan, any temperament. Amount scales the table.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

## Voicing (5)

\phantomsection\label{blk:MidiUnison}\blockicon{voicing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MidiUnison}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[VOI]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Doubles each note into multiple voices, optionally spread across channels.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Mono}\blockicon{voicing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Mono}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[VOI]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Monophonic note priority (last / lowest / highest).

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:MPEConvert}\blockicon{voicing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{MPEConvert}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[VOI]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Assigns each note its own rotating channel for per-note MPE expression.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Slide}\blockicon{voicing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Slide}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[VOI]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Cthulhu's slide: mono legato glue. Picks one held note by Priority (last/low/high) and emits the new note-on before releasing the old one, so a portamento synth slurs between notes instead of retriggering.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\phantomsection\label{blk:Sustain}\blockicon{voicing}\textcolor{neonCyan}{\textbf{Sustain}}\thinspace\textcolor{accentPink}{\footnotesize[VOI]}\thinspace\textcolor{neonYellow}{\footnotesize[MIDI]}\quad\textcolor{textDim}{in: Audio}\quad\textcolor{neonYellow}{out: MIDI}

Sustain-pedal simulation: while Hold is engaged, note-offs are captured so the notes keep sounding; when Hold drops, every held note releases at once. Hold is a parameter, so a CC, an LFO or automation can play the pedal.

\smallskip\noindent\textcolor{neonCyan}{\rule{0.32\linewidth}{0.6pt}}

\hyperlink{blk:AC30}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{AC30}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:AC30}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:Accent}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{Accent}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:Accent}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:AccentEveryN}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{AccentEveryN}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:AccentEveryN}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:AccentOnHigh}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{AccentOnHigh}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:AccentOnHigh}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:AccentOnLow}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{AccentOnLow}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:AccentOnLow}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:AccentPattern}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{AccentPattern}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:AccentPattern}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:Accordion}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{Accordion}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:Accordion}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:AccordionOsc}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{AccordionOsc}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:AccordionOsc}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:AccordionVoice}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{AccordionVoice}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:AccordionVoice}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:Achilles}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{Achilles}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:Achilles}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:AchillesGate}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{AchillesGate}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:AchillesGate}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:Acid303}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{Acid303}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:Acid303}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:AcidBass}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{AcidBass}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:AcidBass}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:Acido}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{Acido}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:Acido}}\\
\hyperlink{blk:AcidVCO}{\textcolor{neonCyan}{AcidVCO}}\,\dotfill\,\textcolor{textDim}{\pageref{blk:AcidVCO}}\\
